msg:
**Repeating bug**

I had reported this issue earlier too. My app seems to go offline on its own in sometime. Whn I switch on the screens and open app it shows 1. Checking for updates and then 2. nodes disconnected and then it connects to nodes in 2-3 seconds. But the task tab remains disconnected and then I have to close and reopen the app to see it connected.
This drops my uptime drastically. Basically I have same phones in same environment (same email id, same WiFi and same 5G networks) but app on this one seems to just go in sleep mode on its own. All battery settings, notifications settings are same in all phones and as they come at the installation of app.

author: Forty41
msg:
Something like this MacroDroid on Galaxy S9 runs as a foreground service with a persistent notification to stay alive, preventing system kills.  Use the Application Closed trigger to detect and relaunch your target app automatically.[reddit +1]
Prerequisites
Ensure MacroDroid has unrestricted battery optimization:
• Go to phone Settings > Apps > MacroDroid > Battery > Optimize battery usage > All apps > MacroDroid > Don’t optimize.
• Grant accessibility permission when prompted (required for reliable app detection).[macrodroidforum]
• Enable the persistent notification (it appears automatically; customize via MacroDroid home screen tile).[reddit]
Create the Macro
Follow these steps in MacroDroid:
1. Open MacroDroid and tap + Add Macro.[youtube +1]
2. Under Triggers, tap + > Applications > Application Launched/Closed. Select your target app and choose Application Closed. (Enable accessibility if prompted; avoid “Force legacy mechanism” unless issues occur.)[macrodroidforum +1]
3. Under Actions, tap + > Applications > Launch Application. Select the same target app.[youtube +1]
4. (Optional) Add a Wait Before Next Action (e.g., 1-2 seconds) under Actions to prevent rapid looping.[reddit]
5. Name the macro (e.g., “Keep App Alive”), tap the checkmark to save, and toggle it ON.[youtube]
Test and Troubleshoot
Close the target app manually; it should relaunch within seconds.  If unreliable, check S9’s Device Care > Battery > Background usage limits and exempt both apps, or add constraints like “Screen On” to the macro.  The persistent notification ensures MacroDroid survives reboots and doze mode.[cardata +1]

It’s Christmas and New Year’s time — for about two weeks, the world just slows down. Nothing really gets done. So let’s give them a bit of time. 👍
They need the chance to demonstrate the strength of their network in order to build real negotiation power. And honestly, some things genuinely improve when you don’t rush them.
If you push ahead too quickly and skip the right timing, you won’t create a sustainable business. At least, that’s been my experience. 😅

author: Byron
msg:
Hello People, happy New Year. Regarding the announcement. So we went from "soon" (3rd of January) to "trust me bro" (no specific time frame). That is ridiculous but not something that I didn't expect. No roadmap, no actual tasks, no revenue whatsoever, no rewards for early supporters, no 2nd selling round. Only pushback of deadlines and "trust me bro" semantics. I made my purchase at the end of September. This is over 3 months ago people! They pitched a production ready product with clients ready to go from day 1. If they now say otherwise, they are simply lying. I attended all AMAs and I understand English very well. Also I am not the only one saying this. Many other invertors state the same. And despite the fact that they never actually claimed a specific revenue from day 1 (like many community members   incorrectly believe and say), a revenue calculator displaying a range of $0 - $96 per license, combined with a production ready product and clients ready to go, translates - at least to my understanding - to an expected revenue of at least $20 from the get go. Definitely not the pennies we are getting now, and we will keep getting for many more days/months as I see it.

The last announcement is not good. Period. If you do not get it, it's on you. I really hope for a NFT marketplace on WMC as soon as possible for all of us who want to sell their NFTs to be able to do so, in favor for way better investment opportunities out there. For now we are just stuck on waiting on false promises and never ending hopium.

author: Mat
msg:
***********This is fiction, I'm just guessing with AI and what we know*********
Scout and Runner:

**Scout**: Identifies where fraud is likely hiding. Selects suspicious routes, carriers, countries, and test scenarios (CLI spoofing, SMS tampering, route manipulation) and triggers targeted test campaigns.
**Runner**: Executes those tests at scale. Sends live calls and SMS through real SIM endpoints, handles high concurrency, captures delivery, latency, and tampering evidence, and feeds clean telemetry into real-time analytics.
**Together**: Scout decides what and where to test; Runner runs the tests and produces proof. This enables real-time detection of CLI and SMS fraud across global carrier routes.

A process might look like this:
1. Scout notices anomalies (or receives a request): “Bank brand spoofing spike in Country X; possible CLI rewrite on Carrier Y.”
2. Scout defines a test plan: origins/destinations, what IDs to use, what success/failure looks like, time windows, volume.
3. Runner executes the plan across endpoints: performs calls/SMS, logs raw evidence.
4. System correlates results into fraud signals (rewrite detected, tamper detected, DLR forgery suspected).

author: Willian | Nuvola
msg:
[Media] **🤝**** Unity Network – Android Update v1.0.8 Now Available
**
The latest Android release of the Unity Network application is now live. Version **1.0.8** focuses on improving operational accuracy, resilience, and user feedback, with particular attention to connectivity awareness and service reliability.

**Download**
• Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.unitynodes.unityapp
• Direct APK: https://releases.unitynodes.io/android/

**What’s new in v1.0.8**
• Proof-of-Work (PoW) status reporting now reliably reflects the device’s actual operational state
• Notifications are triggered on loss of connectivity, providing faster visibility into service interruptions
• Core service stability has been improved across a wider range of device and OS conditions
• Visual refinements and UI fixes improve clarity and overall usability
• Background service persistence has been further strengthened, though some devices may still exhibit aggressive process termination under strict power-management policies

This release continues the ongoing effort to harden long-running background operation on Android while improving transparency around real-time device and task status. Further refinements to persistence behavior will continue in upcoming updates as device-specific edge cases are addressed.

No uninstall is required. This update can be installed directly over your existing version.

Unity Network continues to evolve as a globally distributed, people-powered edge network, with a steady focus on reliability, observability, and operational integrity.

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2007603447709729278

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg: A green status denotes that measured uptime exceeds the lease’s minimum uptime requirement by the defined safe margin of 10%. Leases with a minimum uptime of 95% or higher cannot reach green status, as no safe margin exists. Green status indicates low immediate risk of non-compliance with lease uptime requirements.

author: Stefan Holodnick
msg:
[Media] I’ve put together a solid working configuration for Headwind MDM and built a Docker image to make the setup easier: https://github.com/dailyinvention/hmdm-docker.

This is a recreation of my own VPS setup (which is functional). I’ve tested the image locally, though I haven’t verified the SSL certificate side yet, but it should properly function. I did include scripts to generate the CSR, which you can use with your provider to get a certificate (details are in the README).

A few quick takeaways from my testing:

What works: It’s great for rolling out software updates and direct messaging across a fleet of phones.

The catch: You’ll need to factory reset your phones to integrate them.

Licensing: Advanced features like remote locking require a paid license, which can be pricey, so I’m sticking to the base version for now.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions!

author: Nico | NodeX
msg:
Scout  and runner requires an active SIM:

Scout: “verifies it by activating a SIM card and confirming voice, SMS and data.”
Runner: “operate with an active SIM on the confirmed network” to do calling/messaging/data actions.

Also: Omire said all credits/top-ups required are covered by the job issuer (so you shouldn’t have to fund the SIM usage yourself for the task).

author: Nico | NodeX
msg:
On gamification..

“We are not creating a game.”
They’re adding gamification elements, not turning Unity into a game.
It wasn’t a marketing choice — it came from weeks of internal debate.
The “Scout & Runner” poster/characters are the “front cover” of the first third-party task on Unity’s composable network.

Why use gamification?
To force structure, clarity, and correctness in a task that needs real telecom precision.

The goal is guided behavior:
do the task “the right way”
without vulnerabilities/exploits
without misunderstandings

and with clear “what success looks like” It’s meant to make users feel safe and confident:
no anxiety
no second guessing
no “is this working?” uncertainty

After deciding on gamification, Omri says he brought in a UX gamification expert (designed complex gamified systems for big consumer products, enterprise platforms, and a large educational system) to build the experience properly.

author: Nico | NodeX
msg:
Release plan (fast sprints)

Closed Alpha → more groups (alpha/beta) → public release
Access via registration forms with criteria per group
Community involvement = hands-on testing + structured feedback
Some regions may be blocked initially (regulatory prudence)
Legal teams (internal + external) working “around the clock”

msg:
Hi All! Just joined the verified chat. Seems so much more peaceful here than the public one!

So, I just wanted to put it out there, I think its time Unity Node Operator's are given the oppertunity of a monthly private AMA with the team. I saw Ambassedors are going to get to do that. Is this something we can organise? 

Im not asking for MAJOR secrets, but we need the roadmap, we need some level of understanding of where we are at because I feel like I dont have a clue right now.

Im in the process of organising partnerships/sponsorships and I cant answer the basic questions when asked, It comes across so unprofessional and we only get a first impression once. So, to give us all the BEST shot at a super strong start, this is something that would help.

I want to have everything ready to go when we are past the testing..... but even now, I cant guess if thats weeks, months, Q2,Q3? I honestly have no idea when these kind of business partnerships can effectivly take place.

Is the private AMA something that could possibly happen? WM do it for ENO's and it seems to go well.

author: Daniel 🇷🇴
msg:
I think that's time for a reminder ( @defiants_kay )
**
Core service model:**
1. Your CLI/SMS service tests routes on-demand for paying customers - is that correct?
2. This is different from monitoring live network traffic for fraud - correct?
3. You describe Unity as providing "complementary" fraud detection - complementary to what primary systems?
4. Do you position Unity as an addition to existing fraud management systems (FMS) or as a standalone solution?
5. How does sporadic testing (your model) compare to continuous live traffic analysis (traditional FMS)?

author: Mat
msg:
OK, I wanted to get a little more clarity on the Scout and Runner comms event so used a little speech to text and then queried it a little...
Scout — definition and responsibilities

A Scout is a discovery-and-verification role that operates at the edge of the Unity network.

From the meeting:

What actions a Scout performs:
Operates with a real device, real SIM, and real carrier connectivity.
Uncovers telecom information that exists locally or can be reached remotely.
Targets information that is hard to find and very hard to automate.

Actively verifies discovered information by:
Activating a SIM.
Confirming voice, SMS, and data functionality on the target network.

What signals it generates
Produces verified telecom facts (reachability, network behavior, identity of routes or interconnect characteristics as applicable to the task).
Once verified, the data becomes trusted input for the network.

How Unity uses those signals
Verified Scout data is ingested into Unity as trusted groundwork.
This data enables downstream execution tasks (Runner tasks).
Scout outputs form the first step in a closed operational loop.

Requirements, constraints, examples
Scouts must use real SIMs on real carrier networks.
No improvisation: verification must be exact.
All credits and top-ups required (SIM, calls, SMS, data) are paid by the issuer, not the operator.
If the information is correct and verified, the Scout earns.
Conceptual framing used by the speaker: “Discover” is the Scout phase.

Runner — definition and responsibilities
A Runner is an execution role that acts on trusted information produced by Scouts.

From the meeting:
What actions a Runner performs
Operates with an active SIM on the concerned network.
Executes pre-defined, non-improvised actions, such as:
Making calls.
Sending messages.
Using data in a specified way.
Performs tasks exactly as configured by the issuer.

How it differs from Scout
Scout discovers and verifies information.
Runner executes deterministic actions using that verified information.
Scout work establishes trust; Runner work exploits that trust to perform measurable telecom tests.

What signals it generates
Produces execution results from live telecom activity:
Call behavior.
Messaging behavior.
Data usage outcomes.
Each completed Runner task emits a measurable result and earns a reward.

Requirements, constraints, examples
Accuracy is emphasized repeatedly: “nothing is improvised and accuracy matters.”
Runner tasks depend on Scout outputs and form a handshake / loop with them.
Conceptual framing used by the speaker: “Execute” is the Runner phase.

UNO Operator tasks and expectations
From the meeting, UNO operators are expected to act as hands-on participants, not passive node holders.
Onboarding steps
Register via Unity-provided forms for specific release groups (closed alpha → beta → public).
Meet defined criteria for participation (varies by release group).
Operate real devices with real SIMs on real carrier networks.
Daily or periodic responsibilities
Run Scout and/or Runner tasks as assigned.
Perform real telecom actions (verification or execution) exactly as specified.
Actively test usability and task flow during early release phases.
Interaction with Scout and Runner task families

Scouts: discover and verify hard-to-automate telecom information.
Runners: execute defined telecom actions using Scout-verified data.

Operators may participate in one or both roles depending on eligibility and task design.

What success looks like
Accurate, repeatable execution at scale.
A system that works not just for telecom experts, but can scale from hundreds to millions of participants.
Data that is trusted, usable, and valuable to issuing telecom partners.

author: Mat
msg:
Contextual notes from the meeting

Scout and Runner are described as two characters in a closed loop:
Discover → Verify → Execute

The rollout is phased:
Closed alpha first.
Rapid progression through additional groups.
Frequent communication events planned.

Incentives are emphasized:
Scout earns when information is correct and verified.
Runner earns per completed task.
Issuers cover all operational telecom costs (credits, top-ups).

Quality over improvisation:
Real networks, real devices, real behavior.
Accuracy and repeatability are repeatedly stressed as non-negotiable.

Strategic intent:
These roles are positioned as the foundation for a much larger set of high-value telecom tasks once usability and scalability are proven.

author: Mat
msg:
Here is the Chat GPT shorter version of the session today.
What would people add to better describe this?

Unity 3rd party Task: Scout & Runner

**Scout**
Scouts discover and verify hard-to-automate telecom info using real devices, real SIMs, and real carrier networks. They activate SIMs, confirm voice/SMS/data, and produce trusted signals Unity uses as the foundation for execution tasks. Accuracy matters. Issuers pay all costs. Scouts earn when data is verified.

**Runner**
Runners execute pre-defined telecom actions using Scout-verified data. They make calls, send messages, or use data exactly as specified—no improvisation. Runners generate measurable execution results and earn per completed task.

**UNO Operators**
UNOs onboard via phased releases, run real devices on real networks, and perform Scout and/or Runner tasks as assigned. Success = accurate, repeatable execution that scales from hundreds to millions of operators.

Discover → Verify → Execute.

In Telegram, you can prevent people from adding you to groups and channels by tightening a specific privacy setting. 

On mobile (Android / iOS)
Open Settings → Privacy and Security → Groups & Channels
Set Who can add me to My Contacts or Nobody
If you choose My Contacts, use Never Allow to blacklist specific people

author: SpideyNode616
msg:
Hi All

Following up on my post yesterday, I’d like to propose a private AMA specifically for UNO's?

The goal isn’t just updates, it’s to give the team and UNO's a chance to discuss things that matter for UNO's who are actively building business with Unity, not just investing.

My ideas of topics to cover:

📌 Marketplace & app rollout - when the marketplace and app will be live, so we can confidently communicate to partners.
📌 Desktop UNO portal - makes operations simpler, clearer, and more efficient when we have hundreds, potentially thousands of ULO's to monitor.
📌 Rough timelines - general guidance (Q1, Q2, etc.) so we know when to start conversations with potential partners, sponsors, and collaborators. Right now its Impossible to creat partnerships because we can't tell them if this is weeks, or months away.
📌 Partnership potential - Unity can grow rapidly if the system is simple, rewarding, and easy to use. clear timelines and features help us plan outreach effectively for our own growth, which in turn growns the whole network.
📌 Collaborative marketing ideas - a group of UNOs could potentially pool resources to fund licence outreach (e.g., $100 per node to push thousands of licences through a sponsor. - 200 licences per node, 20,000 licences = $10,000). I personally have sponsorships in mind that I think would get these numbers of licences out quite easily. Would the referral program support this kind of collaboration?
📌 Other questions welcome — these are my personal main points, but any UNO holder can suggest additional topics?

If executed well with partnerships, these 1.2 million licences could go VERY quickly. I have every bit of faith in this, and I think a private AMA could be the start of some very exciting opportunities.

author: Oliwer | I will never PM you first
msg:
**⚠️ Community Safety Notice – Fake Unity Nodes Group**

We want to make everyone aware that **a fake Unity Nodes group is currently circulating and contains impersonators.**
Malicious actors in this group are sharing fake links, which may drain wallets or compromise accounts.

**Huge thanks to Cognito** **and** **Cardano Lady for bringing this to our attention, helping us identify and raise awareness of this issue. Great job! 👏**

**Please take this seriously and act with caution.**

• Only trust official links published on the [**Unity Nodes**](https://unitynodes.io/) and [**Minutes Network**](https://www.minutesnetworktoken.io/) websites.
• Any other links shared in unofficial groups should be considered unsafe.
**• Some real people may have been added to these fake groups without realising it.**

**What you should do now**
If you are in any Unity Nodes group other than @unitynodes or the verified group:
• Report the group
• Leave it immediately

Protect yourself on Telegram:
1. Open Settings
2. Go to Privacy and Security
3. Select Groups & Channels
4. Set Who can add me to:
• My Contacts or Nobody

Staying vigilant protects not only you, but the wider community.
**When in doubt, pause, verify, and rely only on official Unity Nodes and Minutes Network channels.**

If you ever come across fake communities, impersonators, or scammers, please don’t hesitate to let us know.
Looking out for one another is how we keep this community safe, we’ve got each other’s backs 🤝

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: My take is that adoption of the licences and growth of the network is more important than very short term profits for UNOs. To take it to it's logical conclusion, it would be better if a large percentage of the licences were given away at 100% to the ulo for one month than to wait an extra month for the network to expand slowly. Anybody not agreeing can set the percentage at whatever ratio they want and will start to benefit when the Network grows to a point that can support that ratio in the market. Other people setting a lower ratio. Probably just speeds up the point at which your own ratio becomes viable.

author: OC Nodes
msg: Exactly. The first goal is to get all licenses leased and as fast as possible to improve network growth and value. Our bottle neck is getting the 100s of licenses leased first. We make profit by volume no matter what. Of we can make a lot more but it's no use of the network stalls or a customers gets cold feet because they are not seeing strong quantity and diversity of ULOs

msg:
Can agree with this, but for that the leasing options need to be able to roll over & adjust automatically. 

At present, once a lease has expired you need to set up a new one manually, and that’s tedious for some or many, and they may not bother doing that or forget unless rewards are so juicy & of value to them that it becomes worthwhile.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: I would love for there to be a renew option. However, I think that might be a different conversation. I intend to make the default ratio more and more in my favour as revenues increase, sticking to short-term leases. A renew option should probably only be possible where terms haven't changed

author: G Boot
msg:
It’s easy to implement in terms as you state. First Month 100%, month 2 then decreases by X amount. 

I fell the terms are vague & weak too if a client can simply walk away any time, or the Node Owner can revoke a lease with no cooling off period in that respect. So a roll-over or change/update should not be a problem. 

Notification Prompt on expiry:
Do you agree to receiving 65% on your new lease with uptime requirement X and for the duration Y? 

If yes accept, lease roles over, if no decline lease is terminated

author: JP | KUBE |
msg: Or make the ULO pay a termination fee if they break the lease early and based on lease time/time left. Maybe even prevent the UNO from revoking manually and only allow it to automatically when ULO falls below the uptime requirement. That way the lease is an actual one and not just for pretend

msg: Or simply just have a week cool off period If either are uno or a ULO break the lease.

author: BeamNode
msg:
🔥✅Name three things Unity does better than traditional network measurement platforms. (But keep it short)

I’ll start:

**Geo-diverse, continuous edge testing** — Thousands of devices simulate real activity proactively, catching issues at the source.

**No data transmission delays** — Local detection and on-chain logging avoid centralized aggregation latency.

**Proactive vs reactive** — Replaces slow manual or periodic checks with always-on, crowd-sourced validation.

author: Peterjo18
msg:
My ongoing experimentation seems to be indicating that the current tasks have rewards that start falling away after you reach a very modest number of devices coming from the one IP address. 38 devices with high uptimes was giving very low rewards of around .01 per device. 11 devices tethered to a mobile data connection yesterday averaged over .20 per device even though uptime was lower on some devices.
Experimentation is ongoing and I expect the outcome will also vary with the type of tasks the Unity network is pushing out. Currently it definitely seems to be not working well for anything that looks like a phone farm

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: I would assume there is a certain number of jobs currently on the network. Each job pays. As the jobs increase the rewards increase, if the same number of ULOs are active. This then will make it more interesting to run a license, so more people do that. So now the rewards and jobs are split among more people and thus the individual rewards go down.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: BUT, the larger the network, the more interesting it becomes, which then will lead to more jobs/tasks deployed.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: So this will be a back an forth till we reached full saturation of active ULOs.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: But the general direction is up

We onboard ULOs, communicate with them, guide them through the entire process… everything and more.
And then they end up finding absurd 10/90% “desperation” licenses on the market. These licenses will eventually be unbound, deleted, or killed - and the ULOs will lose their licenses as well.

Suggestion:
Do not limit the UNO/ULO percentage when a UNO creates a license.

Instead, set a minimum limit for licenses listed on the marketplace - for example, at least 40% for UNOs.
Otherwise, all marketing efforts at this stage will be undermined by the unserious behavior of individuals offering 10/90% splits.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: Say a licence generates 10 USD per month. At a 50:50 split, say, it is not attractive to people to take. However, a 10:90 split may be just enough to get people interested. Assuming that revenue increases, this is a very short term hump to get over. I don't see how a fixed split helps. It will just delay mass adoption until revenue increases. Having the fixed higher split doesn't alter the point at which you start making money if you want that higher split. It just means that no one makes money until revenue can support that split level. Also, this doesn't seem to have much effect on one-to-one marketing in my experience. People are happy to get my 50/50 split offer on the understanding that I won't rug them

author: Quoia75
msg:
That’s an interesting perspective and it highlights a vital point: one tactic that will be necessary to retain your ULOs is to distinguish your operations from competitors. I’m glad your folks are so far staying loyal. Whatever a UNO can do to make the onboarding process as positive as possible will help. 

However it does not guarantee that some ULOs will not migrate once things start really kicking into gear. The conversion optimization part of my brain is having a hard time accepting paying for an advertising campaign that will have to contend with unnecessary leaks via the marketplace. I may be over thinking it. Everything is theory until put into action.

At minimum, it would be great to have some **brand tickers in the marketplace** to attach to licenses so ULOs can be directed to search for a particular UNO’s licenses.

author: Elias Nikolaidis
msg:
The team never set a device limit, but too many phones on one ISP leads to duplicate tasks, which is worthless to the network. A fixed limit wouldn't work anyway—people would just hit the cap without improving coverage.

We need geographic spread across many ISPs and countries. That’s the only product that attracts Telecom partners and real work. Over-concentration makes the data useless, and the team will be forced to cut devices in those areas. What happens to the farms then?

author: Rdawgie

author: Rdawgie
msg:
Depending on your setup, I've seen a range of rewards you could fall into.
Range 1: $0.01- This could be for many reasons. Bad uptime,  too many phones on 1 IP, bad router configuration.
Range 2: $0.04 - $0.08 Could also be for the reasons above but not as severe.
Range 3: $0.16 - $0.24 Possibly could tweak router setup. Everything else is ideal
Range 4: $0.26 - $0.40 This is the ideal setup/environment. No tweaking required

author: Elias Nikolaidis

This was one of the first issues raised by the Telemachus DAO during the second AMA, and we subsequently submitted a report. As a group of more than two members sharing multiple nodes, a multisig wallet would solve several operational problems for us. Josh acknowledged this and replied that the team would look into it. We understand, of course, that there are other current priorities.
And there are no multisig wallets on the world mobile chain that I know off.. yet...

msg:
Development and growth efforts continue across multiple areas simultaneously:

Customer activity on the network has increased, contributing to higher task volume. This growth reflects the continued adoption of the network's infrastructure by commercial partners.

**Work is progressing on several fronts beyond Scout & Runner**, including the expansion of existing task types, the introduction of new tasks, and the onboarding of early third-party partners building on the network.

**We anticipate continued scaling as additional tasks are deployed - both through internal development** and third-party integrations. Official communications will be published as these initiatives progress.

author: Coffee Nodes
msg:
On the low side, but that’s a separate issue. A minimum fee should have been set post-survey to avoid that race entirely. In any case, no ones really browsing the marketplace yet.

My point still stands: alphabetical ordering does not help ULOs find the best available license. Surfacing the best option at a given time is more rational than neutral ordering. The marketplace should primarily serve ULO decision-making.

msg:
I agree on the minimum fee. It absolutely should have been set. 

The marketplace should be fair for all UNOs as we paid money up front. We are the ones risking our money not the ULOs. So ULOs can look for the best deal if they want to scroll and UNOs have the same chance of visibility. 
In any case, the chance of the more favourable split licenses to go first is still quite high.

author: Eddie H
msg:
The latest version seems to be much more stable. Supported one of my colleagues to become a ULO yesterday, It has literally been set and forget it - her uptime has been great. Ask her if she kept an eye on it and she said no, had not looked at it. To be fair it has only been a few days but still Glad to see real progress with the app not shutting down. 

Personally I have changed my split to 40/60, hopefully get a few people at this level. Will increase later. 
The way I see it, yes I have taken the risk to become a UNO, but ULO don't care about me getting a return.They want to earn some money and we need to get more people using and spending the word. Hopefully a better licence filter would be helpful and a referral program to come into effect soon. 

Hope this makes sense. 😁

author: SpideyNode616
msg:
I write it and use AI to tidy up my wording..... hows that relevant lol?

Bezos bit is about data centres, isn’t saying data centres vanish, he’s saying the centralised model doesn’t scale forever. As compute and intelligence move closer to users (the edge), the internet becomes more distributed by necessity.

In that world, the key problem becomes verification. When services run across thousands of edge nodes, you must be able to prove latency, availability, and performance. Centralised monitoring breaks at that scale. UNITY exists to decentralise verification itself, making edge-first networks provable and trustworthy.

author: Kay | Will never DM you first

author: RaAnRe
msg:
What I was expecting from Unity as manual work was either picking up a call or calling a number or sending a text, etc. If this S&R work requires ULOs to replace their sim (in case of a single sim slot) or buy a new one for testing (even if the cost is paid back), this will might not be acceptable.
For example, in my country, buying a sim card means you need to give details of your national identity, they also check if each individual has a max of 6 sim cards, and other verifications. So for a few $$, going thru this cumbersome procedure just to validate a sim card is not feasible.

author: RaAnRe
msg: Pakistan, Bangladesh, India, Phillipienes, Vietnam.. Sim card process in these places are way different than few western countries. And these are the countries we are hoping ULOs to come from.

author: RaAnRe
msg:
Exactly and in addition to that they also have a limit of how many Sim cards can be registered against one Id.
And the process is not seamless. Even you if you do get the sim rightaway, the activation takes few days and usually the activation is done along with a plan. For example: 30 day, 1.5Gb plan, which will cost more than whatever reward it this task will earn.

author: Nixinzim
msg:
🌐 Summary of the Communication Event
🧭 Purpose of the Session
Provide clarity and progress updates on Unity’s Scout and Runner task system.

Address misinterpretations from the previous communication event.

Walk through the user journey, expectations, and upcoming alpha testing phase.

🔍 Clarifications About Unity’s Readiness
Unity has been built and tested for over a year with telecom partners, carriers, and consultants.

“Building” refers not only to coding but also to:

Legal and regulatory frameworks

Telecom partner integrations

Task translation and delivery mechanisms

The platform is ready, the market is ready, and demand is present now.

Last week’s announcement was not the start of building — it was the start of opening opt‑in tasks to the community and gamifying the user experience to ensure compliance and confidence.

🧩 Overview of Scout & Runner Tasks
These tasks translate complex telecom verification processes into community‑powered actions.

🎓 Academy Mode
Both roles begin in Academy Mode to learn the what, how, and why behind each step.

Includes:

Knowledge modules

A quiz (like a driving theory test)

A practical simulation

Users must be approved by Unity’s admin team before performing real tasks.

Academy Mode remains available anytime for practice.

🛰 Scout Role (Discovery & Initial Verification)
Each country will have up to 50 scouts.

Scouts:

Discover and verify connectivity between their phone (origin) and Unity numbers (destination).

Must use a prepaid SIM or eSIM with international calling — this mimics telecom industry testing behaviour.

Submit the SIM/package details for admin approval before starting.

When calling Unity numbers:

Connectivity Verified → Unity IVR answers with a specific message.

No Connectivity → Any other outcome (disconnect, real person, etc.).

Scouts earn UPs for both outcomes, with higher rewards for verified connectivity.

Verified numbers are then sent to other scouts for secondary validation.

Once fully validated, the number is passed to runners.

🏃 Runner Role (Ongoing Verification)
Runners receive numbers that scouts have already verified.

They must:

Obtain the same SIM/eSIM package used by the scout.

Dial the number and confirm the Unity IVR still answers.

This creates a continuous validation loop.

If multiple runners fail to reach the Unity IVR, the system concludes the connectivity is lost.

Runners earn UPs based on:

Origin/destination

Per‑minute rates

Job issuer agreements

Scouts receive a small percentage of runner rewards for the connectivity they originally discovered.

🔄 Overall Task Loop
Discover → Verify → Execute → Re‑verify  
This cycle continues until connectivity disappears.

🧠 Why This Task Matters
It is a high‑value telecom service being translated into community‑powered proof‑of‑work.

With thousands of ULOs, Unity expects to build the world’s largest distributed connectivity verification network.

Understanding — not memorisation — is essential for compliance and safety.

🚀 Alpha Testing & Release Plan
A registration form for alpha testers will be released in the coming days.

Alpha testers will:

Be selected from multiple countries

Test the user interface and task translation

Provide data on clarity, usability, and performance

If testing goes smoothly, public release will accelerate.

The goal is not to delay — Unity wants Scout & Runner live as soon as possible.

🏁 Closing Notes
The speaker emphasises:

Confidence in the community’s ability to learn quickly

The importance of understanding over parroting

Commitment to frequent communication

Another communication event will take place next week, with Q&A and deeper insights into alpha testing.

[Photo] **🤝**** Unity Network - Infrastructure & Growth Update**

The Scout & Runner rollout represents an important step forward for the network. However, it is one component of a broader, ongoing expansion.

Development and growth efforts continue across multiple areas simultaneously:

Customer activity on the network has increased, contributing to higher task volume. This growth reflects the continued adoption of the network's infrastructure by commercial partners.

Work is progressing on several fronts beyond Scout & Runner, including the expansion of existing task types, the introduction of new tasks, and the onboarding of early third-party partners building on the network.

We anticipate continued scaling as additional tasks are deployed - both through internal development and third-party integrations. Official communications will be published as these initiatives progress.

We remain focused on building sustainable, long-term infrastructure.

author: Elias Nikolaidis
msg:
So simple 
1. The ULO must wait for the official form to be published.
2. Using this form, the ULO will declare a dedicated SIM/eSIM package to the Unity Admin.
3. The ULO will then receive a batch of phone numbers to call.
4. Upon completion, the ULO must submit a report on these calls to the Unity Admin.
5. To ensure accuracy, other ULOs or RUNNERS with identical SIM/eSIM packages will repeat this process and submit their own reports to verify the initial scout's findings.

author: Sadok Merkikou
msg:
Good morning Unity family.

I had an idea, and perhaps you could share it with the team.
You know, like me, that people love the word "free."
But if I had to tell each of my ULOs that they have to buy credits for their lease at a later date, they'd start complaining again.
Isn't it a good idea to let people earn money and then have them pay for the credits based on what they've earned?
People see that as free money, and yet they'll pay for it without a problem.
Perhaps it's also an idea to change something in the application.
A pop-up for the UNO before issuing a license.
A pop-up asking whether they or the ULO will pay for the credits.
If these are transferred to the ULO, a question will pop up asking, "Pay for your credits first to use your license." Of course, this is all for a later date, when people have to buy the credits to activate their licenses, but it's better to think about it now than later.

I'll hear from people if they think it's a good idea or not.

Thanks for your feedback.

author: Eddie H
msg:
I am hoping the market place to be updated so ULO can make more informed decisions. Can filter the split, duration and also whether they will need to pay the fee or UNO.  (Maybe even an option where it states the first month paid for via UNO. )

Being able to have flexibility for different people will be good. A person may want to lock in a 50/50 split for a year duration as in the short term there may not be many available and don't want to keep on changing every 2 mins. Or they say I will go for a shorter duration as they will be able to shop around for better rates later.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: Network telemetry is the automated collection, measurement, and export of real-time performance data (metadata and traffic) from network devices like routers and switches to an external system for in-depth analysis, providing deep visibility into traffic patterns, bottlenecks, security threats, and overall network health. It uses high-speed protocols (like gRPC, UDP) and modern data models (like YANG) to offer much richer, faster insights than older methods, enabling proactive management and rapid incident response, crucial for modern, complex networks.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: See an explanation, what telemetry is, above

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg:
We want to clarify some language around Unity Nodes and earnings. From a legal and regulatory standpoint, nothing related to Unity Nodes should be described as “passive.” Participation always requires intentional action. This includes installing and enabling modules, accepting and maintaining licenses, monitoring performance, and sharing rewards appropriately. These steps matter, and they are part of active participation in the network.

That said, we also understand the practical distinction people are trying to make. Some modules are less time-intensive than others and can run in the background once properly configured, while other tasks require more regular involvement. The key point is that while effort levels vary, all participation involves setup, consent, oversight, and responsibility. We want to be precise, transparent, and aligned with both reality and regulatory expectations.

author: Manuel Orozco
msg:
Discrepancies in Profit Figures: Correspondence with Support.
Overview of Profit Figure Inconsistencies.
I have reviewed some of the comments regarding profit calculations, and after careful consideration, I believe they are not entirely accurate. Specifically, the figures reported by the ULOS system differ markedly from those shown by the OPERATOR NODE (ONE), with ULOS reflecting significantly lower profits.
Contacting Support for Clarification
Due to these inconsistencies, I reached out to the support team on 01/01/2026 to seek clarification. Their response, received on 03/01/2026, is detailed below.
Support Team Response
Hi Manuel Orozco,
"Thank you for taking the time to share this detailed feedback.
We understand your concerns, and you're correct that we are currently in a testing phase. The figures shown in UNO and ULO do not yet reflect final or definitive earnings, and discrepancies like the ones you described are being reviewed.
Since you've already submitted a report, it has been acknowledged and is with the team as part of the items under review. We appreciate your patience while these calculations and displays continue to be refined.
Thank you again for your detailed input — it’s very helpful during this stage.
Kind regards,
Unity Nodes Support Team"
Current Status
On 13/01/2026, I received another message stating that my ticket had been resolved. However, in reality, the issue remains unchanged and the discrepancies persist.

author: ⚡️BeyondLimitsNodes⚡️
msg:
Old telegram group and some Discord chats.

Some basic rules I follow:

1. Tell the truth about current payouts

2. Never talk about the split. Just tell them how much aprox they'll earn per phone

3. Allways make it clear they can use several phones and HAVE to do so under same mail to withdraw in one go

4. Don't go cheap like some 😳 here or you'll end up revoking them. I would hate doing that to poor people

5. Don't check their uptime until you lease atleast 150. It's a waste of time, use the time for marketing instead


6. Target crypto degens and  introduce Unity as Crypto generating APP

7. Answer all ULOs question honestly. They'll feel engaged and could bring you more people


8. Collect old phones when you can to run some licenses on your own, but never pay for them.



9..

10..

Well, doing good so far

author: BeamNode
msg:
#ama

Has the Unity team already looked into possible ways in which individuals or coordinated groups could use the system to their advantage to extract excessive payments, especially with the new third party task ‘scout and runner’?

Can the Unity payment system for this task be compromised if participants intentionally or accidentally perform incorrect actions? Is it possible for individuals or organized groups to exploit weaknesses in the system to maximize payouts?

As the network grows, could people eventually find ways to exploit the system to extract excessive payments, potentially harming its long term sustainability, and are there systems in place or planned to monitor the network and detect such behavior, including coordinated abuse by groups of participants?

[Photo] **🤝**** Unity - New Web Management Capabilities Now Live
**
Unity is expanding its web-based management tools for both Unity Node Operators (UNOs) and Unity License Operators (ULOs), with new capabilities now live - and more on the way.

UNO and ULO network incentives can now be withdrawn directly through the Unity Web Management Portal.
Simply visit https://manage.unitynodes.io, sign in with your account, and you can securely access and withdraw your rewards from anywhere.

The next major update will introduce full web-based UNO and ULO License management. This will allow operators to organise, monitor, and manage all licenses through any modern browser - on desktop, laptop, or mobile web.

These upgrades significantly expand platform compatibility, improve global accessibility, strengthen compliance, and ensure Unity can be used seamlessly across all device types.

More web-based features will continue rolling out as the platform evolves.

author: Sadok Merkikou
msg:
Good morning Unity family.

I had an idea, and perhaps you could share it with the team.
You know, like me, that people love the word "free."
But if I had to tell each of my ULOs that they have to buy credits for their lease at a later date, they'd start complaining again.
Isn't it a good idea to let people earn money and then have them pay for the credits based on what they've earned?
People see that as free money, and yet they'll pay for it without a problem.
Perhaps it's also an idea to change something in the application.
A pop-up for the UNO before issuing a license.
A pop-up asking whether they or the ULO will pay for the credits.
If these are transferred to the ULO, a question will pop up asking, "Pay for your credits first to use your license." Of course, this is all for a later date, when people have to buy the credits to activate their licenses, but it's better to think about it now than later.

I'll hear from people if they think it's a good idea or not.

Thanks for your feedback.

author: A
msg:
Its pretty simple in my view why it wont happen until after round 2 is almost over:

- They changed terms and conditions in the beginning so nodes cant be sold until all nodes are sold or 6 months after sale. 

- The community made them delete that. 

- But they clearly dont want anyone to sell their nodes for 9K when round 2 starts at 10K. Thats why they added the terms in the first place.

- So now that the community made them delete that term, the only option to prevent cheaper sales in round 2 is to delay eth mint until after round 2 or 6 months after the sale like the terms were changed. 

In short they be like: Ok we change the terms back so you can sell your node right away, but we will delay eth mint so you can only sell on WMC were there will be almost no buyers. 

Im not speaking for or against it. For me personally I dont see a reason to sell my node before round 2 is over as the value will double from 5 to 10K. But thats the only logic i can see for eth minting to be delayed and ignored.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: I've tested both Secure Folder on Samsung and Private Space on Google. Simple to set up, reliable in use. They let you keep a UNO login version of the app inside the folder—totally separate—and still run your licensed node-operator version outside, at the same time. Handy because it means no downtime; if you do this on multiple phones it makes for a great backup if the main phone dies or you don't want to travel with your hardware wallet to log into your UNO version. This way, every single phone can earn its keep. Recommended.

author: MSA | Valhalla.Node
msg:
No, Unity can not help keeping X online. At least not in the way it's meant to work currently, and if it ever can, it'll be in a very indirect way. It's testing telecoms routes, it's not changing anything. So best case scenario, it can help detect issues, but the client would still need to fix those issues themselves.

afaik...

author: Nico | NodeX
msg:
List of summary tasks I got from the AMAs...

🔍 Commercial Tasks Prepared
These include advanced telecom verification and quality checks such as:
Call route integrity checks
SMS delivery verification
Caller ID & Sender ID consistency
DLR (delivery receipt) reports
Telecom latency verification
Audio quality scoring (MOS-like testing)

🚀 Next Deployment Phase   (Scout & Runner?)

Telecom environment checks  
Multi-route comparisons across carriers
Increased utilization of SIM + Wi-Fi networks

🧠 New Technology & Task Families

Unity is also developing Wi-Fi network testing technology and has already prepared 10–15 new task families, including:
Multi-hop path analysis
Dual-device synchronized testing
Environment-based verification
Anomaly triangulation
Fraud pattern detection
ISP-level routing behavior analysis
Hybrid Wi-Fi & cellular tasks
Timing & sequence-based testing
Regional pattern verification
Deeper connectivity checks

author: Elias Nikolaidis
msg:
The recent meltdowns have been a real disappointment. We are all here for the same goal.I really can't help but think we could channel our energy more effectively. What if we guided our collective efforts toward developing apps or brainstorming ideas to share and acquire more licenses?

Honestly, we will run out of licenses sooner or later. Our best path forward is to depend on each other—by pooling resources from fellow UNOs or sharing from within our own circle.

author: OC Nodes
msg:
I'm all for positive energy. I'm most joking. Although we could all use way more communication. A project this exciting and investment expensive deserves near daily updates from Dev and commercial team.. 

1- updates about task development 
2- updates about third party plug in apps
3- technical education about telecom tasks so we are all using this time to learn about this sector.. last Unity space they said "we assume you are all experts now in Telecom".. ?? I was taken aback honestly. Like what? We are here on a journey with you. Most of us are not IT and telecom engineers.. educate us and let us grow with you
4- updates about network growth.. daily.. how many licenses online.. where.. 

There are sooo many things they can be sharing. They can be with us here answering questions occasionally.. but no. Dead silence. I mean this is a verified UNO group.. so we are all motivated here together.. I still expect way better from Unity team

author: Gwailow
msg: I dont think it is surprising really - whilst supply > demand, the marketplace will always be a race to the bottom as its just a transaction with no relationship between UNO and ULO. So the only thing UNOs can compete on is price (i.e. high ULO %). Obviously where there is a relationship with the ULO, the UNO can get a better return as price is not the only lever. Letting ULOs have a taste of Unity with a low UNO return isnt a bad thing in these early stages (better than licenses sitting unused). All this changes as demand picks up (hopefully in line with rewards increasing).

List of summary tasks I got from the AMAs...

🔍 Commercial Tasks Prepared
These include advanced telecom verification and quality checks such as:
Call route integrity checks
SMS delivery verification
Caller ID & Sender ID consistency
DLR (delivery receipt) reports
Telecom latency verification
Audio quality scoring (MOS-like testing)

🚀 Next Deployment Phase   (Scout & Runner?)

Telecom environment checks  
Multi-route comparisons across carriers
Increased utilization of SIM + Wi-Fi networks

🧠 New Technology & Task Families

Unity is also developing Wi-Fi network testing technology and has already prepared 10–15 new task families, including:
Multi-hop path analysis
Dual-device synchronized testing
Environment-based verification
Anomaly triangulation
Fraud pattern detection
ISP-level routing behavior analysis
Hybrid Wi-Fi & cellular tasks
Timing & sequence-based testing
Regional pattern verification
Deeper connectivity checks

author: Quoia75
msg:
I read it as having the capability to set a fixed earning amount so for example - $50 a month for ULOs. Or UNOs as the case would be. For me, I'd set a fixed earn for ULOs and the variable profit / loss would be handled on my end.

This would make advertising how much a ULO could make per license a lot simpler and it could also allow UNOs to set up earning tiers.

author: Quoia75
msg: Really looking forward to accessing better management tools via the web portal.👌

author: Nico | NodeX
msg: variable rate proposal is actually very interesting.

author: T E
msg: I think a fixed rate for ULOs could be interesting, but could be more complicated to set up. Less incentive for max uptime with a fixed reward and may only do the tasks up to what they would earn for themselves. Still, I think there would be a version of that that could work in situations.   Just a few more variables to consider.

author: Quoia75
msg:
Hmm - ok imagine this: A base fixed rate guaranteed as long as you meet the minimum uptime requirement. The incentive to meet the uptime is simple: if you don't meet it the license is auto-revoked. This base guaranteed earning would be sustained by the passive tasks. Extra earnings can be gained by opting into the interactive and third-party tasks.

Really would like having this feature. How difficult would it be to set it up? Don't have a clue. Access to a public API would allow people to whip up more bespoke experiences.

author: OC Nodes
msg: Technically at 20c daily per license if you get them all working then you are already making $1200/month. The bottle neck is getting the word out now without a referral program. We will get there. What we are missing is more communication to keep things interesting and allow us to plan better. author: Byron
msg:
who says "there is no rush"? Since us investors and UNOs are expected to do the heavy lifting of marketing/promoting our licenses, we need every tool available YESTERDAY. Tools that the team should have provided from the get go (in the first place - but I do not want to go there again!).

So having the team decouple from implementing these - super mandatory - tools, and give them time to focus on new tasks and revenue, will surely move this project way forward to my opinion.

Regarding GDPR, I do not see how GDPR would be an issue for using the license management API. Of course the service would be GDPR compliant meaning that UNOs and affiliates will need to sign up using their email and/or web3 wallet to use the service and if they decide to stop using it all their data will be deleted.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
I think a problem here would be the implementation. Depending on what incentives the license operators will have for referring someone these are either external or baked into the rewards they get through Unity. If its the latter, then this is something that you probably cant implement with API access 🤔

Do you have concrete ideas how you would set up the referral system? I know the team is still finalizing it and input never hurts 😃 I also find this to be a very important element to get ULOs onboarded and have some ideas how I would implement it. But there are various different routes you could go with this and each has their advantages and disadvantages.

author: Byron
msg:
Exactly, affiliates could be ULOs or not. So you are correct, rewards could target leased license(s) of the UNO(s) they are affiliated with (mainly in the form of increased rev share) or they could be external, where the UNO sends a specific reward to the affiliate directly (if the affiliate is not a ULO, or they have selected the specific reward type). In all cases (and especially if Unity license management API does not provide the option for the UNO to adjust the share of a leased license mid flight) all rewards could be distributed as external (wallet to wallet).

Yes I have concrete and super cool ideas, but I do not generally do things free of charge and I highly doubt that the team will pay for this kind of feedback. keep in mind that this is not going to be a free service (a small commission to UNOs would be applied for every new referral they acquire using the service)

author: James_WM_ENO
msg: I thought it might work like the rewards may be split 3 ways.. say you set a licence at 50/50 then when one of your ULO’s find someone they can generate their own code that will send the next person to one of your licences, then perhaps a small % like say 2% would go to the original ULO, and 48% to the new ULO

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
Thats my preferred style as well. 

 ULO referrs another ULO and gets a share of their rewards. This increases their rewards on which they then pay the predetermined share to their UNO. 

If a refferd ULO reffers another ULO, then the referrals rewards slowly creep up the chain. 

1. This would incentivize each ULO to get refferals, because you earn most on your direct referrals.

2. This would also incentive a ULO to make sure their referrals are actually active, because their reward is based on the revenue their referrals get.

UNO splits will increase as demand increases.  Just my view

author: Mat
msg:
Ok, as we don't have a roadmap or any real dates, for my own sanity and a bit of fun here are my predictions on dates.
1. Roadmap 20 Feb
2. iOS PoW 26 Jan
3. S&R first week of Feb for Alpha, mid March for general release.
4. New stand alone passive tasks Valentine's day but limited release and lottery to start with.
So lots to look forward to, will just take a little time.

**Scout & Runner:** Final preparation underway. Alpha registration to be released in the next 48 hours, and further details to be announced this week.

#ama

What if someone uses a rooted Android phone to clone the Unity app ten times on a single device? That would lock up ten licenses for months due to the contract.

Does Unity actually detect this? And if it does, can those devices truly be banned? Otherwise, revoking a license just means they take another one. (Or lower the rewards to $0)

If Unity does not detect this at all, how is a UNO supposed to detect that multiple licenses are being used through cloned Unity apps on a single phone?

[File: Scout & Runner Alpha Registration.mp4] 🤝 **Unity Network - Scout & Runner - Alpha Tester Registration is Now Live**

Alpha tester registration for Scout & Runner is now live.

Scout & Runner is powered by Unity Network and introduces connectivity verification tasks to the network.

Who can apply:
• Existing Unity Node Operators (UNO)
• Existing Unity License Operators (ULO)

Requirements:
• Daily availability during the Alpha period
• Willingness to provide detailed feedback
• Ability to follow testing instructions

Apply now: https://unitynodes.io/alpha-registration/

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Access is limited and will expand as part of the phased rollout of Scout & Runner.

This is an Alpha test - participants should expect bugs and interruptions. Your feedback will directly shape the production release.

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network – v1.1.2 Release Now Live**

The latest Android release of the Unity Network application is now available.

Version 1.1.2 introduces new marketplace functionality, real-time notifications, and the first opt-in verification task.

Download
• Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.unitynodes.unityapp
• Direct APK: https://releases.unitynodes.io/android/

What's new in v1.1.2
• Marketplace Enhancements - Filters added, listings now randomized with daily reshuffling
• License & Lease Details - Additional information included on listings
• Notification Framework - Users will now be notified of important events in near real-time
• Opt-in CLI Verification Task - Validate Caller Line Identification integrity by receiving inbound calls and reporting CLI data for route verification
• UI/UX improvements and additional housekeeping

No uninstall is required - install directly over your current version.

This release marks an important step forward, introducing the first opt-in task to the network. Unity Network continues to expand as a globally distributed, people-powered edge network.

Join the distributed edge network. Secure your Unity Node and download the Unity Network App at unitynodes.io

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2014337947911287117

author: Rick Middleton
msg:
I can sympathize with people calling for a free market - but IMO this should only be done if it is impossible for a UNO to cancel a Leased License unless the ULO has dropped below the agreed uptime.  

Otherwise it will result in unscrupulous UNOs cancelling the minute the average market price starts to rise.   Bad for ULOs. They might decide not to bother.

Also when I've told a few people about Unity face to face they seem to think 50/50 is a fair split.

I think it might depend on how passive or active the ULOs are.  If they have to do a lot of actual work it seems like they deserve a higher % then if they do nothing and their phone just does it automatically.

author: Joshua F
msg:
Hi all, I want to share a thought based on my experience onboarding new ULOs (especially non-technical ones) to simplify the UX. What if the default in-app flow had no marketplace and just used simple standard rules:

 • All licences are 60% UNO / 40% ULO and if no referral is used one is selected at random from all available (no marketplace).

 • Each UNO has one referral code. If a new user enters it, they claim a license and get +10% rewards uplift  (50% UNO / 50% ULO)

 • All lease lengths are infinite as long as 80% uptime is maintained; UNO can only revoke if uptime drops below that.

 • UNOs can still set any UNO/ULO split which can be claimed manually with the longer codes we use today.

Why: avoids race-to-the-bottom, massively simpler for non-technical users (don't need to understand UNO ULO or marketplace - just that they earn 10% more rewards by using a code), easier for UNOs to onboard beyond our immediate circles, strong incentive for UNOs to start marketing.

author: Brandolf [NOVA]
msg:
The issue is that it will be harder to onboard ULOs at these early stages with these low rewards if the splits are controlled, essentially giving less incentive to the ULOs.

If it is harder to onboard ULOs, the network will grow much slower, if it is growing slower, telecom customers who pay for the width and depth of the edge network will pay less, it won’t be that attractive to them.

Think of it like this: What is a better deal 10% on $100 or 50% on $2?

author: Byron
msg:
Hello people. I have read all messages regarding the marketplace and the minimum share. Let me give you my two cents. I believe its wrong for us UNOs to argue regarding what is going to happen to the marketplace. Simply because, at up until now, at least, we do not have any kind of actual useful data to work with. The team knows and configures everything. The supply (tasks) but also they have the full dataset of the demand (leases, type of devices, locations - so can easily correlate leases with average wage etc).

All it takes for the team is a couple of smart marketeers to dive into the data and regulate the market so as to achieve the best possible  performance. I believe they should run some models and decide on an algorithm which will update the UNO/ULO dynamically based on market structure/KPIs (e.g. percent of leased/unleased licenses, type of devices (wifi only, sim enabled etc), farms or physical devices, ULO locations and of course type and number of tasks available at each time). The goal of the model would be to maximize revenue for both UNOs and ULOs alike of course.

So the marketplace, for me, should be a regulated market. A dynamic UNO/ULO split should be applied for any new license a ULO is taking from the marketplace. UNOs will not be able to change that for their licenses available on the marketplace. Of course we will be free to take our licenses off the marketplace and apply whatever split we like for our own ULOs.

author: Byron
msg:
An unregulated market will still find equilibrium but in our case specifically will certainly take way more time to do so (due to the lack of information to the participants) compared to a fully regulated one.

With a fully regulated market and the team pulling the ropes to give UNOs the best possible ROI and ULOs the best value for their efforts I believe we will achieve the best possible results in the shortest time frame.

author: Quoia75
msg:
Question for  the "Free Market" folks - since Josh clearly stated that UNOs are responsible for bringing ULOs into Unity, what do you think is a fair solution if only part of the UNO population is paying for marketing, while the rest sits back and waits for these same leads to migrate to their 10/90 licenses? 

If Unity were actually a passive investment I frankly wouldn't care about marketplace pricing. If I had access to a white label version of the app that didn't feature the marketplace, I also wouldn't care about marketplace pricing.

Since I have to put work into marketing it on top of the initial outlay in cash, I want to maximize my return while making sure ULOs also get an an enticing share.

author: Tristan B
msg:
Yeah I think there is room for discussion on market minimums, but we are so far from what we are all envisioning for this project it seems like it’s too soon to set limits.

If someone sees they’re getting 50:50 on $5 they might not see it as worth it, spread fud, etc. Which will only hurt Unity as it’s trying to grow. Might be easier for people to sign on to that if they’re still making $20-30 bucks at the end of the month, I don’t know… but I worry about setting any limits before we really see an increase in rewards.

I think more the more
Important discussion is figuring out the rules for setting licenses and revoking them if they’re haven’t already been outlined. As that has an impact on the splits we will be comfortable with setting.

author: Willian | Nuvola
msg:
🤝 Unity Network – iOS Update v1.1.2 Now Available

The latest iOS release of the Unity Network application is now live on the Apple App Store.

Version 1.1.2 introduces marketplace improvements, real-time notifications, and the first opt-in verification task available on iOS.

Download on iOS:
• https://apps.apple.com/us/app/unity-network-app/id6755482738

What’s new in v1.1.2
• Marketplace enhancements - improved browsing with filters and daily randomized listings
• License & lease details - expanded information available directly within listings
• Notification framework - near real-time alerts for important account and network events
• Opt-in CLI verification task - participate in Caller Line Identification integrity checks by receiving inbound calls and reporting observed CLI data
• UI/UX refinements and general stability improvements

No uninstall is required - simply update over your existing installation.

This release represents an important step forward on iOS, bringing opt-in task participation and improved marketplace visibility to Unity Network operators worldwide.

Secure your Unity Node at unitynodes.io and become part of the distributed, people-powered edge network.

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2014508954676580698

🤝 Official Unity Resources
Unity | Unity Litepaper | QR Code Login | Manage | Telegram | X | YouTube | Bug Report | Verification Bot

author: UNO
msg:
Hello, my fellow UNOs, after reading all the messages and arguments regarding the marketplace, thought I share my view.

I believe all UNOs along with the Unity Team collectively own the marketplace and it's fair that we should collectively decide on the rules of the Marketplace.

Not all UNOs would be good in marketing and we need proper marketing to onboard all the ULOs who will run the licenses which in turn will generate rewards for the Unity Network as a whole. So we need to incentivize who are doing the marketing.

After thinking a lot on this topic, here's my take on this matter:

Marketplace Split: UNO 50% / ULO 50%
Why? This feels more like a partnership between a UNO & a ULO.

Anyone who brings a ULO, gets a 5% referral bonus taken from the UNO's share as long as that lease is active.

Once a ULO leases a license, a direct communication channel is setup between the UNO and the ULO. Using this they can then renew the lease based on another split if they so choose.

There should be an option to auto renew the license at the end of its term.

A UNO should not be able to revoke a license, if a ULO is meeting their uptime requirements. Let's say if a ULO is not meeting their uptime requirement for 5 consecutive days, the license gets automatically revoked and made available again in the marketplace.

If a ULO leases a license and does not bind it to a device within 24 hours, then the lease automatically gets revoked and made available again in the marketplace.

author: Gwailow
msg: 100%. If you're spending time cultivating relationships to be ULOs, then they are less likely to take a better offer from the marketplace. If potential ULOs are shopping on marketplace then that will be purely price driven as there are no other levers that a UNO can use .. but where they have a relationship and comms channel then the more engaged UNOs should be able to demand a higher %. I agree a few features like being able to establish contact with a ULO coming from marketplace, ability to autorenew leases, a cooling off period after revoking or cancelling a lease (both UNO and ULO) etc will help but I dont think minimum UNO/ULO splits is the way to go.  Im perfectly happy taking 14-20% at the moment from someone who picks up a license from the marketplace with no effort from me.  As demand increases, the UNO % will increase too.

Currently, when you disable the task your number is marked as inactive - and inactive numbers are cleared from the database after approximately 1 month. Re-enabling the task will mark the number as active again until changed.

author: BeamNode
msg:
So if I understand correctly, caller ID testing works as follows.

 1. You receive a test call and you do not answer it. This is intentional because only the displayed caller information is being verified.
 2. You open the Unity caller ID testing section to report the call you just received.
 3. In the app, you see the caller ID information that Unity expects to be shown, usually the phone number and sometimes the caller name.
 4. You compare what was shown on your phone screen with what is listed in the app.
• If the number shown on your phone matches the expected number, you mark it as correct.
• If the number shown on your phone is different, missing, or altered, you mark it as incorrect.

author: Quoia75
msg:
🌞Good Morning.

The adverse to this POV is there are UNOs who are ready and willing to launch full fledged marketing campaigns. This activity will cost $$$. It's not greed or short sightedness to prefer not to give away your marketing budget while others sit back and collect on the proceeds. 

If I were wealthy I'd give every UNO a $3000 stipend to help them boost there promo efforts. I'm not wealthy therefor I have to be concerned with ROI for every marketing dollar I spend. Giving away ULOs to 10/90 UNOs after you've paid to acquire them is not part of an efficient marketing plan.

If we weren't told in a past AMA that we are are also responsible for attracting license operators, this would be an (almost) totally moot point - yes, just let the free market decide. Establishing a marketplace minimum is not the most elegant solution but it will inject some equilibrium and the fact that it's being decided by a community vote is better than some arbitrary floor decided upon by the Unity team.

author: Byron
msg:
I believe is an overkill for the ULOs to have to search the marketplace for licenses with better terms. Remember people we want MASS adoption, non crypto, non tech savvy people, your ordinary, friendly neighbor have an app in their phone that runs in the background, does not bother them, and they are making FIAT money. That was the pitch and initial vision.

The app usage should be freakily stupid simple. Login -> enter a lease code (if you have one), browse the marketplace for a license or the app will assign an available license for you. Your are good to go. Your only concern? keep your device connected 24/7 and you earn .. that's it.. mass adoption.

The ULOs do not have to know anything about UNOs .. splits ..  uptime percentages and lease durations. All that drive mass adoption away from this app. The usage of a license must be completely transparent to the ordinary ULO.

For this to be fair, for everybody, the marketplace must be regulated. An automatic UNO/ULO share must be applied on all licenses in the marketplace. As I have stated yesterday, that share must be adjusted constantly based on market conditions. Also, having different uptime thresholds is unnecessary to my opinion. The team should define a minimum uptime for all licenses in the marketplace since they define the SLA between the network and telcos. Last but not least, regarding the license lease duration, UNOs should be able to adjust that but this should also be transparent to the ULO where when a lease duration ends the system should assign them a new license from the marketplace or (if none is available) auto renew the current one.

All automated and transparent to ULOs. Just register and start earning. Just keep you phone on!

Outside the marketplace? UNOs can do whatever they want with their licenses.

author: CloverNodes
msg:
I’m voting 60% UNO / 40% ULO
Here’s why I think this is the best long term move:
This is a marketplace minimum not a cap. UNOs can still do personal distribution however they want. A higher marketplace minimum gives UNOs real margin to do word of mouth + local marketing which is the most effective way to drive adoption. If UNOs don’t have enough upside, they won’t push growth. If they do they will scale distribution and everyone wins. 60/40 creates good incentives keeps it fair and builds a healthier marketplace

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: Contingency theory tells us that the decision should be dependant on the specific circumstances at a point in time. The problem we face is too many unknown and uncontrolled variables such as revenue at different points in time, whether it will be possible to revoke licences before the end of term if the conditions have not been broken, whether people taking licences off the market will know whether or not the UNO will pay for the licence fee etc. My preference would have been a free market, which is the best way of dealing with uncertainty. Given that we are selecting a minimum split then I suppose it has to be orientated towards a steady end state and we have to guess what revenue that equates to. In this circumstance I feel we have to assume the project will be a success and so I will have to go for a higher Uno split. I am also persuaded by the argument that a higher split for unos encourages individual unos to market directly. That is my tuppence worth.

author: Byron
msg:
to all of you who think that 60/40 is not a powerful tool for the marketeer. Let me tell you a couple of things about customer psychology, or better yet, marketing tactics. It's a powerful tool in marketing to be able to offer to your potential buyer a 50/50 split. equal shares hits hard in a person's phycology because it triggers confidence vibes due to the other party proposing equality. So UNOs being able to offer a 50/50 split when the higher rate a buyer can find for themselves in the marketplace is 40, trust me is a big deal. And they wont think that its only 10% better, its the equality that hits hard their phycology and are way more keen to purchase your licenses.

The team want's us to market this thing. They have to give us the tools, and we have to help ourselves to be able to do our part as effective as possible. Given the circumstances a 60/40 minimum marketplace split will provide us with a powerful tool to market our licenses, and drive adoption more effectively that with a lower minimum split

author: Laszlo
msg: For those who are curious, it's the minimum split for UNOs on the built-in marketplace on the app. For example, if you vote 50/50, none of the licenses can be below 50 for UNOs on the marketplace. However, you can still market your license for a better split to your ULOs and decide the split. This feature prevents a race to the bottom and gives UNOs room to market themselves. I voted 60/40 (if you already voted, it's still possible to retract your vote and revote)

author: Laszlo
msg:
Unity Vote Stereotypes

40/60 Voter: "It's just a side hustle, baby! I'm already financially free, so let's onboard everyone, the marketplace will handle the magic while I sip pina coladas. Zero stress, max growth!"

50/50 Voter: "Fair is fair, right? 50/50 sounds super balanced, like splitting a pizza. Not totally sure what this vote does, but hey, equality vibes!"

60/40 Voter: "I get it, I'm the marketer now! Over-invested? Guilty as charged, but I'll recruit ALL the ULOs to this life-changing project. Need that ROI to fuel my dreams... let's build this empire! 🚀"

for those with 🔱's... it's a fun take 😜

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: A 60:40 split leads to one of two things happening for UNOs. 1. You put your licences marketplace and get a decent return (a win). 2. You market your licences independently and can out compete the market (a win). The argument against this is that it can lead to slow adoption. Until rewards are good, there will always be slow adoption and any adoption will be driven by Uno marketing. Help UNOs with their marketing. Vote 60:40

author: Quoia75
msg:
People are already taking up licenses with 50/50 splits and even higher in favor of the UNO. Some in this group are just reluctant to accept that we are partially responsible for marketing these licenses.

Just a gentle reminder (again) all UNOs will be free to set whatever split they want on their private leases. Price controls in marketplaces is not something new or exotic. It's the bread and butter of wholes/retail. Without it markets collapse and influential wholesalers (us) aren't motivated to put forth their best effort to move product.

author: Quoia75
msg: I believe this will change once UNOs start investing in marketing. This will support the efforts already being undertaken by the Unity team.
