author: James_WM_ENO
msg:
**Unity Network -** **Q1 2026 Roadmap**

The Q1 2026 priority roadmap for Unity Network is now available.

The following roadmap outlines priority targets and is subject to change. Timeframes are indicative. Items may be adjusted, reordered, or rescheduled based on development requirements and network priorities.

- Special UNO Event Release
- Scout & Runner Alpha
- iOS Proof of Work
- Web3 Partnership Releases
- Planned UI/UX Improvements
- General House Keeping
- WiFi & SIM Hybrid Powered Task
- Communication Events
- Scout & Runner Public Release

author: Joshua F
msg:
Hi all, while onboarding ULOs (especially non-technical users) I’ve seen the marketplace confuse people and sometimes stop them completing setup. I've submitted a feature request to simplify the default flow:

TL;DR: if splits have a minimum as per the latest poll and licences are effectively surfaced by an algorithm anyway, the marketplace layer may be redundant and adds friction. A simple “claim” system could improve onboarding for everyone.

Feature proposal:

This proposal replaces the default marketplace experience with a simple in-app licence claim flow designed for new users. On first use, a user either enters an optional referral code or skips it; if they skip, the app randomly assigns a licence from the available pool at a standardised 60% UNO / 40% ULO split. If they enter a valid referral code (one unique custom code per UNO), they claim a licence and receive a +10% rewards uplift, making the effective split 50% UNO / 50% ULO. Licences are effectively indefinite provided the user maintains at least 80% uptime, and the UNO can only revoke if uptime drops below that threshold. Separately, UNOs can still publish custom UNO/ULO splits that users claim manually using the longer codes we already use today, keeping advanced flexibility without forcing it into the default user journey.

It’s needed because the marketplace adds avoidable friction and decision fatigue at the exact point where non-technical users are most likely to churn. Standardising the default split eliminates race-to-the-bottom dynamics and prevents incentives from drifting towards short-term undercutting. A one-tap claim flow makes onboarding materially easier for people outside the existing crypto-native circles, while the referral uplift creates a clear, measurable incentive for UNOs to market and actively bring in new participants. In short, it improves conversion and retention by simplifying the path to first rewards, while preserving customisation for power users via the manual route.

author: Quoia75
msg:
*Caveat - __this request included an additional option to auto upgrade or downgrade ULOs through earning levels before they would have a license auto-revoked. There was also a more plain vanilla version that just requested having an auto revoke feature.__

— Submitted November 6, 2025 —

**Enable automatic tier upgrades and license revoking based on up-time requirements**
After an operator sets up several earning tiers (i.e. 30%, 50%, 70%, etc.) they will have the option to also tie these payment tiers to a required percentage of up-time for the licenses to be retained. 

Examples below:

__Tier 2
Earning potential: 50%
Up-time Requirement: at least 50% up-time for 60 days__

If the licensee falls below this requirement for a consecutive number of days (let’s say 5, this should be customizable) then the license will be auto-downgraded to the next lowest tier. If they continue to fall below the required up-time threshold for each tier they will eventually have their license revoked and they will have to request a new one (lowest tier).

Conversely, if they are meeting the up-time requirements for their current tier, they should be auto-upgraded to the next highest earning tier (example 80% for 60 days). As long as they are able to maintain a threshold of good uptime for the highest tier, they will be rewarded by being able to keep the license for perpetuity. 

In this scenario, all licenses are made available at the lowest tier first, and excellent uptime is rewarded by upgrades to a higher earning tier, and being able to retain the license indefinitely.

author: Byron
msg:
No unfortunately you do not get it. Marketing wise we must start high and move lower. The other way around never works, except when we are a monopoly or the demand greatly outperforms supply. Fortunately this will be the end game for Unity, but until we get there, marketing wise, we need to start high and have the option to promote lower, "special" deals.

Furthermore, since in the early stages, UNOs will drive adoptions with their efforts, they  need to be incentivized. If we were in a state were rewards were high enough so as this thing to market itself, I would agree with you. But we are not there, and manual (UNO) actions need support.

Do not forget, the team has stated that they expect from us, UNO, to promote this thing. At least, we should allow ourselves to do what is expected from us as effectively as possible. Not voting for the 60/40 is like shooting your own foot, marketing wise. This will only result in dragging the project adoption down until it can eventually market itself.

Think about it.

author: Laszlo
msg: For those who are curious, it's the minimum split for UNOs on the built-in marketplace on the app. For example, if you vote 50/50, none of the licenses can be below 50 for UNOs on the marketplace. However, you can still market your license for a better split to your ULOs and decide the split. This feature prevents a race to the bottom and gives UNOs room to market themselves. I voted 60/40 (if you already voted, it's still possible to retract your vote and revote)

msg:
Unity Vote Stereotypes

40/60 Voter: "It's just a side hustle, baby! I'm already financially free, so let's onboard everyone, the marketplace will handle the magic while I sip pina coladas. Zero stress, max growth!"

50/50 Voter: "Fair is fair, right? 50/50 sounds super balanced, like splitting a pizza. Not totally sure what this vote does, but hey, equality vibes!"

60/40 Voter: "I get it, I'm the marketer now! Over-invested? Guilty as charged, but I'll recruit ALL the ULOs to this life-changing project. Need that ROI to fuel my dreams... let's build this empire! 🚀"

for those with 🔱's... it's a fun take 😜

author: Byron
msg:
regarding the "external" marketplaces .. that I have read about the last few hours. This is totally ridiculous. What part of MASS ADOPTION you do not understand? Let me say it one more time.

In order to achieve mass adoption the ULO needs to be onboarded in just 2 steps:

1) Enter their email to register
2) Validate their email, and automatically get assigned a license from the marketplace and start PoW

All other options have to be optional (enter affiliate code, enter license code, browse the marketplace) and preferably out of the "mainstream" onboarding process.

For mass adoption, only one marketplace matters. The default Unity marketplace. Anything else will be for the initiated, which will be the vast minority of ULOs.

Mass adoption means —> Download Unity app from Apple/Play store, register and earn
Mass adoption does not mean —> Download Unity app from Apple/Play store, go to site A, browse for a license with a "benefiting" profile (learn about UNO/ULO splits, uptimes, lease durations), get the license code, register to the Unity app, redeem license code, start eanring

author: Mike
msg:
I don't really follow all the 60/40 supporters, saying it'll allow UNO's to market for a lower split...
That way UNO's who do extra work get less rewards than UNO's who don't market themselves.

Meanwhile without a minimum (or a lower one), the licenses that get listed cheaply, will quickly get picked up, automatically raising the floor.
This lets UNO's, who want to do extra marketing, to do so for a higher split. This means the UNO's who do more, will get higher rewards.
You could even argue that it makes their job of marketing easier, because there's already an existing network of ULO's that are getting great rewards.

author: Quoia75
msg:
I'm not understanding the "hard work" stance here or the comparison that keeps being made between the input of a UNO or a ULO. Both are vital to the network. Both play a specific role. And lets be honest, installing an app, maintaining good up time and completing a few manual tasks is not like toiling in a mine. 

I think this POV is coming again from mistakingly believing that UNOs are passive investors. 

This POV also diminishes the outlay of work the UNO had to produce to acquire the initial investment needed to purchase the nodes to begin with. Just based on a few stories I've read within this community, I'm assuming most here are not wealthy and had to make some sort of sacrifice to purchase a node or nodes. That's time & money invested.

Depending on the quality of the customer care you intend to provide to your ULOs or the effort you will put forth in marketing, the UNO role will involve some actual work and expenditure. It's clear the quality of the UX will vary from UNO to UNO.

author: Depins
msg:
I wasn't a fan of this marketplace cap. But I said let's see how it goes. Since this is going into effect, how can I use it to my advantage 

For starters my licenses weren't on the marketplace yet. But now since terms are capped I figured might as well release them into the wild.

50/50 got my first lease in lease than 24 hours. The device is bound and their uptime is increasing. 

So that is encouraging.

Also this will make marketing easier when I decide it's time for that.

So my advice is to give it sometime and see how it can work for you and the network as a whole.

author: OC Nodes
msg:
I can start to see the light a little bit behind their reasoning. Again I see a little bit

The logic I suppose is that this marketplace is only the official unity marketplace.. and in reality with mass lease creation and referral system , you can set your own terms.

And it's true in a way this protects you because you can tell your customers.. look the marketplace's best option is 60:40 while I can do 50:50 .. for instance

Of course the downside is that you have to have your own market and action plan

But again, once rewards pick up, your listed licenses in the marketplace will still get snagged


My real issue is that this will slow adoption and the low rewards now will turn people away

Sure they can take your 50:50 or 60:40 lease now and keep the uptime, but what is the retention rate?

We need as high of a retention rate early on as possible for Unity to reach organic traction and growth ..

I think the market limit can hinder this

The only way this works is if the rewards are really going to scale up soon

author: Depins
msg:
Pros and cons. Everyone can't be happy. 

Fact is, people that are marketing currently are somewhat disadvantaged by having a marketplace undercutting them. 

By capping the marketplace those marketers are encouraged and incentivised to continue marketing. 

It also encourages others to market as well. 

All of these marketing efforts will have overflow. The overflow benefits Everyone including those not marketing. 

Suppose you wanted to use the market and do 10/90 to undercut everyone and "compete"
Now if your licenses are capped at 50/50, just 1 license leased is worth what 5  would be worth if leased at 10/90. 
So you retain more of your license supply. Bot bad considering you're not marketing yourself 

And you still can actively market as well. 

So the free market purest that doesnt want to do marketing is only disadvantaged in that they can't get all their licenses leased before everyone else by using aggressive pricing strategies 

But technically they still can they just have to be a little clever about it and put in some work. 

It's not possible to make everyone happy. Not having a cap now would be disadvantaged to those currently making an effort to bring in more ULO'S. 

I think this structure is worth giving it a chance.

author: SpideyNode616
msg: America isn’t a fully free market anyway, a lot of the time prices are controlled or heavily influenced. Prescription drug prices are negotiated or capped for Medicare. Interest rates aren’t free-market, the federal reserve sets them, which affects loans, mortgages, and savings. gas, electricity and telecoms can’t charge whatever they want, regulators approve or cap prices. So prices aren’t purely supply and demand, the government has a hand on the dial. So, staying on the topic of free markets rather than being a dick and criticising other peoples countries, America isnt the free market you,re suggesting it is. This is the same.

author: Gwims
msg: You know I don’t believe these low uno leases would remain if a license suddenly starts making 1k a month.  My initial baseline is 70/30 privately. Information is key I think I even tell them about the market place, the splits that are available there and give that $1000 scenario. I explain that more licences will mean more money over time also each additional license I lower my share by 5%  and I cap it at 55% to uno. Even with all that my baseline on market place is 70/30 and my licences are being taken even with those 10 90 splits around. The problem with those splits is I know UlOs will get rugged people that have done it have said as much in previous threads I’ve read. As Calus said there are different users of the licenses some are content to sit back and get the passive, they won’t be that bothered with opt  in tasks. Others they will be on it, those guys I normally say to them start with 5 licences I paint the picture of a potential once everything is established. I also set people’s expectations from the beginning giving a longer time frame and what current rewards we are getting.  It’s a business we are running ourselves at the end of the day some will depend on market place some most of my licences are strangers I’ve just spoken to around me. Lol from my experience close friends and family ask you a million questions and still don’t download the app. I think all this will change when rewards are more significant, the first part the building is always harder

author: Kyle | I will never PM you 1st
msg:
Currently, when you disable the task your number is marked as inactive - and inactive numbers are cleared from the database after approximately 1 month. Re-enabling the task will mark the number as active again until changed.

We are updating the UI to be more clear - There will be a toggle for the task itself, which will apply the behaviour above, and an option to opt-out, which will remove your number.

msg:
Okay jokes aside. I’d love to know your opinion on this very important topic:

If we settle on 60/40 split for the marketplace, there shouldn’t be an option to withdraw a license when contract conditions are met by the ULO.

Imagine an UNO leasing out his licenses at 60/40 or 50/50 whatever with 65% uptime and 12 months lease when the license is generating $1 UP per day. Then a couple of weeks later new active tasks arrive and the potential to earn $5 UPs or higher per day per license increases but only if the ULO does them actively. Should the UNO be able to cancel the lease or not?

Please share your thoughts 💭

author: Calus B
msg: The only reason I’m suggesting that a higher minimum be implemented is that the current app marketplace is in the app. This means that a UNO could do no work at all to promote the network and grab an early share of the new users coming on board. This lowers the incentives to actually spread out the network. If the second you get a new user coming in and they just grab a bunch of 1090s from the marketplace and you’ve spent the time and effort to get them there. That’s very disheartening. Any UNO that wants to do the work and offer lower numbers can absolutely do that. They just can’t rely on sitting there sniping other people’s users. Additionally, if someone else wants to build a marketplace, I would be 100% for that. The only reason that I think the an app marketplace should be kept is because it’s the lowest possible effort at least with the other marketplace unity no operators would have to promote people to go there and get it.

author: Brandolf [NOVA]
msg: All contracts are binding. Imagine you rented a house or a flat for a year and due to a cool civil project or a very important factory that will provide the region you live in with good paying jobs etc which moves the rent prices higher where you live, should your landlord arbitrarily cancel your lease or adjust the rent accordingly? This doesn’t make sense. It’s your choice to not set a lease time at 12 months, but if you do you are bound to it. Think twice before signing a contract - that should be a universal understanding and logical

It's a critical period where we need to attract customers.. can't be rolled out slowly at leisure. Loss of customers can kill the project. I also understand why they can't tell us who their customers are as competition can go and disrupt these relationships.. so we need all hands on deck, all the time. We need maximum expansion in order to achieve more attractive network for customers.

author: Laszlo
msg:
Unity Vote Stereotypes

40/60 Voter: "It's just a side hustle, baby! I'm already financially free, so let's onboard everyone, the marketplace will handle the magic while I sip pina coladas. Zero stress, max growth!"

50/50 Voter: "Fair is fair, right? 50/50 sounds super balanced, like splitting a pizza. Not totally sure what this vote does, but hey, equality vibes!"

60/40 Voter: "I get it, I'm the marketer now! Over-invested? Guilty as charged, but I'll recruit ALL the ULOs to this life-changing project. Need that ROI to fuel my dreams... let's build this empire! 🚀"

for those with 🔱's... it's a fun take 😜

msg:
Hi Folks. I am new to the group. I want to share my thoughts on the current poll. I am trying to think holistically as much as possible as possible about the pros and cons of all options for uno/ulo split. The main tipping point for my 60/40 uno/ulo split is this… 

If we have a 60/40 it will allow UNO’s to privately set contracts for 55/45 and 50/50. This will provide  retention and promotions for individual UNO’s as the marketplace will be set at 60/40. This is absolutely critical to retaining your customers. Please consider this as you vote.

author: Zolee
msg:
With the 60/40 split I would have the incentive to advertise both the marketplace and a better split. 
If a user takes my license with a better split, I’ll earn less money I’m fine with that. If I drive a user to the marketplace and they take a license for 60/40, I’m also fine with that as I’ll earn more if I get lucky and they choose my license by chance.

My flyers promote the marketplace firstly and give an option to users to get access to help to set things up or they can get answers to their questions directly from me if they run my licenses. 
I put massive amount of work every day into promoting both the marketplace and my licenses. 

Those who are unable to market due to lack of ability or interest will benefit more from my efforts than if the split was 50/50 as I’d not want to put in so much effort.

author: CloverNodes
msg: The people I have onboarded with a 50/50 split have stuck around and are happy to wait for increased rewards. What stops them right now from jumping ship to a lower split? author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
I think a higher split heavily favours those UNOs that already have a folowing or have the marketing capabilities. I understand the reasoning, but the same could have been achieved with 50/50 or even 40/60. Lets say the $96 come true eventually and you have 80% of your licenses leased out with a 30/70 split. Thats still $4.600 PER MONTH a UNO would get...

I personally think thats enough and that a higher ULO split has a way better chance to go viral, because people earn more in a short period of time.

There are obviously two different sides here and we will never know what side would have been "right". You can make a compelling case for both IMO. But if its decided by this "vote", then it will be decided like that and we have to accept it 🤷

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
Thats your assumption and that might be true for some of those advocating for 40/60 or less but certainly not for all. I spend a great amount of time marketing my licenses so far:

Twitter
Reddit
TikTok
Build 2 dedicated TG groups
A custom signup form
Several Email templates to engage with the ULOs, depeding where they are from
Spend hours on making a visualy appealing onboarding guide
Provided countless hours of 1on1 support for my ULOs
started a private referral system
and so on.

While I was doing so I managed to get 14 licenses leased and activated. So taht around 3 hours per activated license so far. Meanwhile, lots of people have gotten licenses taken from the marketplace. I personally havent gotten one taken from there. However, I am still advocating for 30/70 because I think thats a fair split. I bought into Unity not only for my own personal financial gain, but also to change lives for my ULOs. And with 30% I feel I still get enough to have a comfortable live. But thats MY POV and everyone is free to have a differing opinion.

Regarding the spllit: Even with a split of 50/50 or 40/60 UNOs are still incentivized to do marketing if they want. BTW: You could even make an argument that UNOs who want a higher share should convince theier community/ULOs by offering a superior service compared to those who just put their licenses on the in-app marketplace. Also, I think a GOOD REFERRAL SYSTEM would be the most powerful marketing instrument, alongside sufficient revenue. And that revenue is achieved more easily with a higher ULO split 🤷

Everyone will have a different opinion on this and I dont think we will find a solution that fits all. So thats why we have this poll (which isnt a vote, but okay) and I will accept the outcome. I probably wont like it, but I will accept it, because thats what grown ups should do 😃

author: CloverNodes
msg: Thank you for your counterpoint and no I am not suggesting that everyone must become anything. Unity as a whole has gotten to where it is because of the success UNOs have brought to this project. There are countless people in this chat who have done an incredible amount of work to onboard ULOs and that is what I am advocating for with this minimum. I want to see the people who put in the work win. Now to answer your other question about people who have full time jobs and cannot really take the time to market, this is exactly why a higher minimum can benefit them too. They can simply list their licenses at a higher split and earn more once rewards and demand increase. But for that demand to increase we need ULOs and I am sure you agree they are the driving force here. In order for us to reach more ULOs we need word of mouth and we need a push from UNOs. So no, I am not suggesting everyone go full time on World Mobile, Minutes Network, or even Unity. I am saying the people who want to put in the work need to be incentivized the most and I firmly believe those same people will be the main drivers of growth in this initial phase. The rest who want to set it and forget it will have to wait or find another way to onboard ULOs. This is a sharing economy after all, work needs to be done in order for this to work.

author: Matej
msg:
This is not how serious projects are governed.

If the vote was publicly presented as __final__ and explicitly stated that it __will be adopted__, reopening it afterwards fundamentally undermines trust, credibility, and the legitimacy of any future governance process. A vote cannot be “final” only when the outcome is convenient.

Concerns and objections should have been raised **before** the poll, not after the community invested time, effort, and good faith into constructive discussion and decision-making. Changing the rules post-vote signals indecisiveness and poor leadership, and it sends a clear message that community input is conditional rather than respected.

If results can be disregarded or re-voted whenever they are uncomfortable, then the process itself becomes meaningless. Governance by polls only works if outcomes are honored.

The realization that a Telegram poll was not the appropriate mechanism should have been made __before__ declaring the vote final, not after the outcome was already established. 

And despite all, keep in mind that only verified UN owners participated in the poll.

I strongly urge the team to respect the original vote and adopt the agreed 60/40 split as stated. Anything else sets a dangerous precedent and seriously damages confidence in the project’s decision-making framework.

author: Byron
msg:
This is ridiculous. We demand clear explanations for this. You say "based on community feedback" .. WHAT community feedback? The community is here, in this group, we voted two times, we argued and only a miniscule minority mentioned that this is not a proper voting system. HELLO??!!!! we all new that this was not a proper voting system, but its what we got to get the job done in a period where new TASKS is of UPMOST importance!

The VAST MAJORITY of your community in here, did NOT express concerns about this process not being a proper voting system. We accepted this and followed along. Your excuse that "community feedback" driven you to cancel this whole process and decide to implement something COMPLETELY UNNECESSARY at this stage, is at least INSULTING to the vast majority of your community.

YOU knew from the beginning that this was not a proper voting system, and yet you decided to do two rounds of this. You had your community vote twice. And after the two rounds you figured out that this is NOT a PROPER voting system? What do you thing we are? a bunch of mindless monkeys that you clap your hands and we all dance around you?? This is a shame. I feel totally betrayed by the team.

Your decision proves - for one more time - that you are TOTALLY disconnected from your community.

YOUR community HAS spoken. YOU need to HONOR their decision. Whether  you like it or not.


PS.
And if you do not believe me, wait and see how many upvotes, my comment  is going to get.

author: Gwailow
msg:
#ama Before we go for yet another vote - can we get some clarity from the Team on what legal review has been completed on the concept of UNOs voting to set a minimum revenue share in the marketplace? 

The more I consider it, the more concerned I am that any such move could be deemed illegal in UK/US/EU under anti-competition/antitrust law. I am only familiar with the detail of the UK version (EU is very similar) but there is an argument that collusion and agreement between competitors (UNOs in this case) to fix the price (the ULO share) and provide the ULO with less choice or power to make independent decisions on which licenses to lease, would be in breach of the law. Collusion would be pretty easy to prove here given the 1000s of TG messages debating what level the price should be fixed at. The price in this case would be the financial arrangement of the ULO revenue share (unclear if this is an issue - sharing UPs only but UPs are redeemable for dollars).

I don't want to be a killjoy here but before we go any further, can we get some confirmation that this has been considered and cleared by the team's legal advisors?

author: Calus B
msg: I think because we are not limiting but an individual UNO ™ does it doesn’t get covered. Any uno can give any split. It’s only on the default marketplace that a minimum is set

author: Coffee Nodes
msg: also customers don't pay, they just earn less. So it's not "price fixing"

author: Gwailow
msg: "It’s only on the default marketplace that a minimum is set" - this is where my concern lies. The default public marketplace is potentially limiting customer choice and setting standard terms.

author: Gwailow
msg: im pretty sure the financial terms of the lease agreement would get caught in the definition of 'price'. Maybe not given that we're only sharing UPs. But would prefer that this is determined through a legal team rather than us all guessing.

author: Mat
msg:
@defiants_kyle Can this question please be added to the backlog of questions for the team and/or be added to #amaquestions:

As a UNO, my core responsibilities is finding ULO's & keeping them engaged and active on the network, it's their participation that is essential for generating revenue across the network.

The concern I have is how clearly UNOs will be informed about the actual production activation date of each task, not just when the task appears in the app or becomes visible to clients, but when it is genuinely live from a ULO’s perspective.

We’ve just seen this issue with the “Caller ID Testing” task. It was “launched” and appeared in the Unity app four days ago, yet it isn’t actually receiving call requests from clients. I communicated this task to my ULOs at launch, and now they’re asking why no calls are coming through. Without clarity on activation timing, ULOs are getting frustrated, trust in the project weakens, and engagement becomes harder for all of us; the UNOs, the team, and the wider network.

**Question**: For future task releases, will UNOs be given a clear production-activation date, the date when the task will actually begin generating work for ULOs, so we can engage and inform our communities accurately and maintain confidence and help them remain active and engaged in Unity?

author: Quoia75
msg:
Your interest in this topic is appreciated. Considering UNOs are being potentially cast as "franchisees" you might find the following materials informative. Skip the AI scrapebots (Grok, ChatGPT, etc) and go directly to these sources:

- (USA) FTC Franchise Rule – Statement of Basis and Purpose / Official Interpretive Guidance
- (USA) Sherman Antitrust Act, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1–2
- (US) Leegin Creative Leather Products, Inc. v. PSKS, Inc., 551 U.S. 877 (2007)
- (UK) Competition Act 1998
- (UK) Enterprise Act 2002
- (UK) UK Vertical Agreements Block Exemption Order 2022 (VABEO)
- (UK) CMA Guidance on vertical agreements and the Chapter I prohibition

If there is a pre-dominance of US or Western case law in these examples, it's simply because these countries (the US in particular) tends to have an oversized influence on how pricing law is written global.

Is it illegal to set an RPM price for a product? It depends on country and jurisdiction.

Of course, nothing above is legal advice and is provided purely for education purposes.

If you want to place this source information in a AI bot for further study I'd recommend Gemini or Perplexity over Grok and make sure to keep the bot from hallucinating or inferring by adding the following prompt to your query:

"**CAUTION: Do not infer. Only base your response on existing case law and primary legal sources**."

HTH👌

author: Hello Pal (Connected Earth)
msg:
Sorry to bring this topic back. I have been loosely following the debate on the split between UNO and ULO. I was for one towards the lower UNO and higher ULO split. My licenses are at 30:70 at this moment. But I do understand the other side of the argument. Unfortunately, with the current 60:40 consensus, the marketplace is practically dead. 

I think there is a middle ground we can rally behind with a small tweak. 

Rule:
60:40 split for the first $10 per license / month. Thereafter, UNO will be allowed to set their own split.

$10 number can be $15 or $20. I have just put some arbitrary number to convey the idea.

Benefits:
UNOs advocating higher split: They are still incentivized to do their own marketing.

UNOs advocating lower split: This group may not have time to do their own marketing. They can still rely on marketplace.

ULO: They are incentivized to cross that $10 to get into higher tier bracket. 

I really hope community gives it a thought and decide on this common sense approach.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY

author: Quoia75
msg: ...Except for subcontractors usually perform a task for the contracting party behind the scene completely under the hiring contractor's umbrella. For example, Built Well, LLC, a construction company, needs to put up a house for a client. Instead of keeping all labor in house, they pull together a pool of short-term subs to get the job done, but it's all being done under Built Well, LLC's brand name. The client will only ever see Built Well on all invoices and billing statements.

author: Quoia75
msg: With Unity you will have thousands of UNOs, some sticking strictly to using the Unity brand, others creating their own branding while distributing Unity product under their own labels. Unity has charged UNOs with marketing Unity and we have not been given any strict marching orders about how to go about that or been required to sign Teaming Agreements or Non-Disclosure Agreements that legally explain how we are suppose to conduct ourselves while representing the Unity brand as we market.

author: Quoia75
msg: For tax purposes, none of us have been required to sign any documents that state we are sub-contractors (W-9 status in the US.)

author: Byron
msg:
I am sick of this lack of meaningful communication and "play with words" game. The team obviously think we are mindless plebs that they can play with.

What we all have to understand is that the power is in our hands. This thing is nothing without us, not only because we paid, but also because we offer the decentralization.

We should dictate the rules and priorities. NOT the team. We paid, our money they are using, and we provide the decentralization. Without us this thing is DEAD.

So, for me.

- The team's first priority is COMMUNICATION with US
- The team's second priority is to implement tasks and evolve the product

I am willing to remove all my licenses from the marketplace and stop all promotional/marketing actions until the team behaves and starts treating us like partners. I urge everyone in here, who has invested their hard earned money for just being played (remember the voting fiasco right?), to do the same.

We have the power to change this. Shut down your farms, big or small. Stop promoting. Lets see how long they will keep ignoring us.

Think about it.

author: Don Standard
msg:
Building on the discussion above, here’s how I’m currently thinking about it. 

Setting an artificially low ULO profit share on the marketplace won’t prevent undercutting. It will simply push competition off-platform into Telegram groups and DMs. 

That still results in marketeers losing ULOs they onboarded, while new users are flooded with offers and exposed to bad actors. It also creates a real risk of scams, which reflects badly on Unity as a platform. 

Another unintended consequence is that, if organic leasing becomes harder for some UNOs, it may incentivise consolidation of devices rather than growth of a distributed operator base — which runs counter to the goal of a broad, decentralised edge network. 

A cleaner solution would be for Unity to apply a consistent, platform-defined licence split wherever licences are offered. If no one can offer preferential terms elsewhere, there’s no incentive for DM outreach, licence switching, or spam.
People who put real work into marketing Unity would still have a fair advantage by being able to distribute their own lease codes. Those who don’t wish to, or are unable to market, would still have their licences fairly allocated to new ULOs who join without a code. 

This isn’t about restricting competition — it’s about clear, transparent rules applied equally, protecting users, and keeping activity on the platform rather than in private messages.

author: Byron
msg:
Thank you for your comment. I totally get your point of view and your arguments. Let me clarify a couple of things.

1. I was among the first, probably THE first, to say that the whole voting thing was useless. I thoroughly explained my reasoning and suggested an automated system which would guarantee fair incentives (shares) for both UNOs and ULOs and at the same time allow us to boost marketplace performance and product awareness UNITED, since there would be NO need for individual offers. The optimal share (in terms of revenue and adoption) at any given time would be the one already set by the algorithm, so any deviation from that would directly mean less performance, something that no sensible UNO would want. The marketplace can move to a fully unregulated status when it reaches a certain capacity/maturity in terms of supply (tasks) / demand (quality ULOs).

2. Given the decision from the team for a vote, and a hard fixed UNO/ULO share I had to propose what I believe best fits our tasks as marketeers. What would give us the most tools to promote our licenses LONG TERM, not only when incentives are miniscule as right now. I am sorry if I had you divided. That was not my intention. My only intention is to help in making this project, my investment, successful and rewarding, as soon as possible.

author: Akira
msg:
**TL;DR – Unity License Distribution Proposal**

The current license marketplace forces UNOs to compete against each other for ULOs via reward splits, fragmenting marketing and hurting long-term growth.

This proposal removes the marketplace entirely. Licenses are automatically assigned to ULOs when the Unity app is installed and a license is activated, using a neutral allocation method.

To keep strong marketing incentives, UNOs are rewarded through a referral system: if a user installs the app via a UNO referral and keeps a license active for a minimum period, the referring UNO earns a reward. No instant payouts, no split bidding.

➡️** Result**: less internal competition, aligned incentives, simpler onboarding, and rewards tied to real, long-term adoption rather than short-term tactics.

If this sounds good to any of you and want to see the full proposal in detail, DM me 🤝

msg:
I didn't read the full proposal yet and I appreciate the debate, but I have problems to understand how it's better than the one that imposes a 60:40 cap and practically kills the marketplace.
For what I read from the posts it suggests to develop an algo that, when a potential ULO approaches the marketplace, randomly picks a license among all available ones applying a fixed "unified" split rate. But let's say that the rate will be the 60:40 that came out from the polls.
If I understood correctly I think we can save the cost for developing the algo and the picking system because even if the team applies the poll decision of a minimum 60:40 split ALL the licenses that will be made available to the marketplace will have exactly the same 60:40 split creating a "de facto" unified split rate as suggested by this latest proposal. 
It's just that the randomization will be performed by the ULO himself during the picking.

What is inconceivable is that, because some influencers already leased licenses to friends, family and followers with a "very generous for themselves" 50:50 the whole marketplace must bend the knee and be set in a way (60:40) that doesn't disturb their business, despite other UNOs have different business plans. 
This proposal seems not fixing this issue

I agree on a referral code that rewards UNOs that forwards potential ULOs to the marketplace. I suggest to consider even a slice of rewards (5%ish on license's UNO gains) for the whole lifetime of the contract

author: CloverNodes
msg:
Your proposal is great and I have to give you credit for taking the time, it solves a lot of issues but it introduces some fundamental changes that need to be addressed:

**1) Automatic assignment breaks the incentive loop:** The proposal removes the marketplace and does automatic assignment on install (round-robin / load-balanced / performance weighted)
 The issue is simple, if I bring a user to Unity and they get assigned to someone else why would I market? The proposal tries to solve this with referrals later but that still creates a “marketing dead zone” in the meantime.

**2) It removes ULO choice and community trust:** This proposal makes onboarding “simpler” because users no longer choose operators. But many ULOs want to pick an operator because:
A) They trust them
B) They want support
C) They’re part of that operator’s community

Random assignment makes UNOs interchangeable. That kills relationships, loyalty and the most important part it kills retention.

**3) “Protocol-defined” becomes political fast:** Akira says it would be neutral and protocol-defined. But once you introduce:
- performance weighted assignment
-uptime scoring
-quality metrics
you’ve created the next battleground. Now everyone fights over the algorithm instead of the marketplace.

**4) Referral systems create a new type of abuse:** He does include safeguards like:
-time-locked rewards
-one-time attribution
-caps / decay
-device fingerprinting + wallet -uniqueness + rate limits

That’s good, but in practice referral systems attract:
-fake installs
-churn loops
-phone farms
-referral laundering
So you may remove split wars, but you introduce referral warfare.

**5) The timing is backwards:** In the doc, referrals really “pay” when the network reaches capacity and licenses are scarce, like via airdrops. But incentives are needed most RIGHT NOW, while adoption is still being built. You don’t remove the main incentive today and promise rewards later.
Akira’s proposal is thoughtful and I agree with the problem it’s trying to solve, race-to-the-bottom splits are not healthy. But removing the marketplace entirely breaks the incentive loop for UNOs to market, it also removes ULO choice and it shifts conflict from splits to an algorithm.

**The better fix is simple:** keep the marketplace, set a minimum standard split, keep private deals free market and add referrals as a bonus layer on the ULO side to drive adoption.  That keeps builders incentivized today while still eliminating the worst marketplace behavior.

i love that the proposal is actually picking up interest from the community and that these discussions are taking place

you make valid points too, addressing the main one: marketing incentives for UNOs

in my initial draft, UNOs that actively market unity app would be directly rewarded with a % of the revenue of all licenses from ULOs that installed the app thru the UNO referal link (that is, after a certain period of activity)

but discussing the draft with dodo he proposed that receiving more licenses from future airdrops could be a bigger motivation to keep growing the network, and i agree when thinking long term

i think both options (earning a % from referals vs. getting more licenses in future airdrops) can co-exist, and actually both could be implemented at the same time. 
sort of: short term/immediate incentives + long term growth incentives

author: CloverNodes
msg:
Referral systems sound great on paper but they always attract people trying to game it. Fake installs are when someone downloads the app just to trigger a referral reward then never use it.

Churn loops happen when people activate for the minimum time.. cash out whatever reward then drop off and repeat.

Phone farms someone runs 20 to 200 phones to simulate “new users” and farms referrals which is hard to combat 

Referral laundering are when groups trade referral codes and recycle signups to make it look like real growth, even though it’s just the same circle of people. So you might remove split wars in the marketplace but you replace it with a different battle who’s is people competing to exploit referrals instead of competing to provide better value.

The proposal does mention time-locks, one-time attribution and Sybil resistance which helps but it’s still a real risk in any referral-based system which in my opinion needs to be thought of very well before being introduced

author: Joshua F
msg:
Dont want speak for Akira but I’d answer these as follows:
1. This point is made null by the minimum split that will be enforced after the next poll. There will be almost zero differentiation between licenses and assignment will be based on the algorithm putting random leases at the top of the marketplace. Best option IMO is drop marketplace and just do random license assignment so that its un-gamable.
2. Also believe this point is null until there is far more in-app UNO social functionality like UNO support and leaderboards. 
3. Same as point 1
4. All these issues are avoided with minimum uptime requirement and license revocation. My suggestion ontop of Akiras model is to start by using referrals to lease your own licenses by dropping default 60/40 split to 50/50, therefore ensuring the UNO doing the marketing benefits directly, and so does the user USING the referral code who gets a 10% rewards boost.
5. Same as previous point, UNO benefits from having their licenses leased which even at todays rates is a win.

Ultimately, the proposal sets up the UX to be much more beginner friendly to those new to the ecosystem and prevents churn at the most critical stage, whilst incentivising mass marketing from UNOs.

author: CloverNodes
msg:
Love this reply and I’m glad you clarified it because that addresses my biggest concern which is the “incentive loop” for UNOs marketing. I actually agree that both can co-exist… short term incentives + long term incentives makes sense.

My only pushback is this:

If we remove the marketplace and rely primarily on future airdrops as the long term incentive it can become too delayed and too uncertain for most UNOs to justify real marketing spend today. Most builders won’t grind 60 to 90 days or longer on a “maybe later” reward even if the vision is solid. Also referral reward in the form of future licenses is a good idea but it still introduces questions like:
how often airdrops happen?
how the weight is calculated?
How quickly UNOs feel the payoff?
what happens if the network pace slows?

So I’m aligned with your direction but I think the safest version is this:

Keep the marketplace with a minimum standard split preferably weighted towards an UNO initially so 60/40 split (so we stop the race to the bottom) keep private leasing fully flexible and add referral rewards on top on the ULO side (both % revenue and future license weighting) That way we don’t break anything that’s working today but we still evolve into the more cooperative model long term.

Really appreciate you pushing this conversation forward I gotta run but I’ll reply back on any counter points later on just tag me.

msg:
Bruh, I wouldn't call it panic. Just the next critical thing that **really** needs to be addressed before the big push to the public.

Here's an UNO trying to recruit ULOs:

"Hey! Wanna try this app that lets you earn $XX a month in extra income? Oh btw, it only pays in crypto. Oh, yeah if you want to withdraw you can only withdraw in these 5 cryptocurrencies. Oh, also, you need to set up your own crypto wallet. Oh by the way, there are sooo many scams in crypto right now, here's some education to keep you safe! Oh, that 24 words your wallet creates? That's called a seed phrase - when the scammers start calling you make sure you never ever give it out! Here's how to keep you safe, I'll teach you about hard wallets too....How do you withdraw? Oh, that's right, go to this separate link outside of the app."

Yup, nothing to be be concerned about here.🤟

Def a work-in-progress.

author: Jamie King | Minutes Network
msg:
[Photo] **Unity Network - Unity Marketplace Final Community Poll**

**UNO holders, please note** 👇

Following the conclusion of the recent Telegram polls, we are now moving into the final stage of community polling on the minimum UNO / ULO split for Unity Marketplace leases.

**The two options included in the final poll are:**
60% UNO / 40% ULO
50% UNO / 50% ULO

**What to expect:**
Eligible participants will receive an email invitation.
The email will be sent to the email address used for your Unity Node order.
Participation is completed via the Unity website.
You’ll receive a one-time passcode (OTP) by email to verify your identity.

**Polling window:**
Opens: Now
Closes: Sunday, Feb 1st at 7pm CET

**Important notes**
This applies only to public Unity Marketplace leases.
Each participant may submit one selection only.
Submissions cannot be changed once completed.
Results will only be shown / published after the polling window closes.

👉 If you don’t see the email, please check your spam/junk folder before raising a support ticket.

Thank you to everyone for taking part in this important final step in shaping Unity Marketplace lease terms.

-The Unity Network Team

author: 🇰🇼 Ibrahim Almaraghi
msg:
Not a legal expert myself 

But i do work somewhere where i get a good exposure to legal stuff in a daily manner with other experts, so what i am about to say is "logically driven":

If the project itself is setting a minimum split on their own platform, and NOT forcing any split outside of it (like putting penalties on the users if they do so outside the market place" then it "should" be fine, 

For example:

Scenario 1:
"We are putting a minimum split on the official marketplace."

Ok, that's fine.

Scenario 2:
"We are putting a minimum split on the official marketplace AND You are not allowed to trade outside our marketplace or you will be penalized for it"

That is not fine.

One does "still" have freedom of choice.

The other is pure manipulation.

A minimum split will only put "extra" efforts on the UNO side of things if this was implemented.

Again, I'm not a legal expert, and rules do/may differ from one country to another, but in a basic sense on legal matters, a minimum split "shouldn't be" illegal if imposed on project's own market place only.

author: Gwailow
msg:
I have a different viewpoint on this which is where my concerns stem from. 

The default, official (and currently only) marketplace is establishing a common financial arrangement for leases through the marketplace that limits the free choice of the potential ULO and limits competition amongst UNOs. For a regular person interested in becoming a ULO, their first port of call is likely to that marketplace as its directly available from the app - so its difficult to say that the ULO has any choice on the financial aspect of any lease they take through the marketplace.  Saying that UNOs can set their own terms is fine but this is looking at it from a UNO perspective - someone who just downloads the app and wants a license is unlikely to be in a position where they can contact a bunch of UNOs to get the best deal for them.

The danger here is that everyone is thinking from the perspective of a UNO and not from the potential ULO. It would be hard to say that setting a minimum does not limit competition or the freedom to choose from the ULO perspective. The one argument that I think could stand up is whether or not this is a financial arrangement but its a very grey area given that UPs are convertible to USD.

author: Gwailow
msg: Free market pricing may not be restricted but access to that range of pricing is restricted (or at least not readily and obviously available).  That for me is where the issue lies.

author: feqifei 🇮🇹 [COOL] [CALM]
msg: Moreover, it is not accurate to claim that UNOs can create their own marketplace with the same functionality as the in-app one, due to the lack of public APIs that would allow UNOs to manage licenses in an equivalent way. For instance: they cannot automatically lease a license. Instead, they must manually generate an activation code, so it needs personnel 24/7. They are even unable to check whether a license is already leased, which prevents them from properly removing it from their own marketplace

This concern is understood and has been carefully considered in the design of the Unity Marketplace process.
The minimum UNO / ULO split will operate only for public Unity Marketplace leases within a closed, permissioned marketplace. author: Forty41
msg:
Gemini is much more helpful . Evidence for the involvement of **World Mobile** and **Minutes Network** in **Unity Nodes** is found in official project documentation, corporate partnership announcements, and joint whitepapers.


Unity Nodes is a collaboration designed to create a decentralized verification layer for the telecommunications industry.


**1. Official Documentation and Project Websites
**The primary evidence is hosted on the official [UnityNodes.io](https://unitynodes.io/) website and its legal terms.
**Terms of Service:** The [Unity Nodes Terms and Conditions](https://unitynodes.io/terms-and-conditions.html) explicitly state that the company collaborates with **Minutes Network Token X (MNTx)** and **World Mobile Treasury Services Ltd (WMTx)**, identifying them as the "Third-Parties" that provide the underlying infrastructure and rewards.
**The "Unity Bundle":** Every purchase of a Unity Node Software License includes a "complementary allocation" of both **MNTx** (Minutes Network Token) and **WMTx** (World Mobile Token), directly linking the financial and operational health of the node to both projects.  
**2. Corporate Governance and Leadership
**The leadership teams of both companies are publicly linked to the Unity initiative.
**CEO Involvement:** Micky Watkins, the CEO of World Mobile, and Josh Watkins, the CEO of Minutes Network (and former CIO of World Mobile), have both officially promoted Unity Nodes as a joint effort to decentralize telecom auditing.
**Press Releases:** In late 2023, Business Wire published an announcement detailing Josh Watkins' move from World Mobile to Minutes Network, describing the two companies as "entirely complementary." Later announcements in 2025 formally unveiled Unity Nodes as the "fruit of a collaboration" between them.   
**3. Technical Infrastructure (On-Chain Evidence)
**The technical "Proof-of-Work" for Unity Nodes relies on the infrastructure of both networks:
**World Mobile Chain (WMTx):** Used as the **settlement and traceability layer**. When a Unity Node performs a verification task, the critical metadata is hashed and recorded on the World Mobile Chain to create an immutable record.   
**Minutes Network (MNTx):** Acts as the **operational layer**. Unity Nodes verify the global voice traffic handled by Minutes Network's proprietary "MinTech" technology.
**Summary Table: How They Connect
Component**
**Project Involved**
**Role in Unity Nodes**
**Settlement Layer**
World Mobile (WMTx)
Cryptographically anchors verification proofs on the WMChain.
**Operational Layer**
Minutes Network (MNTx)
Provides the voice traffic and "Switch Nodes" that Unity Nodes audit.
**Reward Pool**
Both
Node operators are rewarded in tokens from both ecosystems (and others like BTC/ADA).
**Where to Verify Yourself
The Unity Rewards Paper:** Available on the Unity Nodes website, this document breaks down how fees from both networks flow into the Unity ecosystem.  
**The Minutes Network Whitepaper:** Often references World Mobile as a key partner for data settlement.
**Official Socials:** Check the verified X (formerly Twitter) accounts for [@WorldMobileTeam](https://x.com/WorldMobileTeam) and [@MinutesNetwork](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://x.com/MinutesNetwork)[,](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://x.com/MinutesNetwork) where joint AMAs and announcements regarding "Unity" are frequently posted.

author: Quoia75
msg:
Honest answer - if you already have three and you have at least $10k in emergency money set aside, I would be content with that. 

If you have disposable income and a strong marketing and license distribution plan in place and you're just waiting for the app to have essential features implemented and for more tasks to be released, I'd considering buying another 1 or several.

Ultimately, no one else can tell you how to spend your money. Just treat every investment as a potential loss and don't use money that is essential to your survival to buy into high risk ventures like this one.
